issue_title: Percent is not encoded correctly in paths
issue_body:
When constructing a path, a reserved character like space is encoded correctly by furl: 

```python
>>> furl('http://localhost').add(path=['foo bar']).url
'http://localhost/foo%20bar'
```

However, a percentage character is not encoded (and the following two characters are capitalized):

```python
>>> furl('http://localhost').add(path=['foo%bar']).url
'http://localhost/foo%BAr'
```

In this case I'd expect the output to be:
```python
'http://localhost/foo%25bar'
```

issue_title: Handle Invalid IRC Password
issue_body:
Some IRC servers need a password when you are joining.

This is handled in [irc.client.py](https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/irc/src/1612fffff20b95715d6fb822dc9fb182176951cb/irc/client.py?at=default) line 493:

```
#!python

  # Log on...
        if self.password:
            self.pass_(self.password)
        self.nick(self.nickname)
        self.user(self.username, self.ircname)
        return self
```

This sends the **PASS aPassword** command to the server before **NICK** and **USER**. 

This is all following perfectly to the [RFC on passwords](http://www.irchelp.org/irchelp/rfc/chapter4.html#c4_1_1).

The problem arises if this password is _invalid_. 

I cannot see how the server tells us if the password we just sent was bad. I don't see it handled in the flow of the code in client.py and my tinkering around seems to show the program just sits there doing nothing if this happens. 

Can an error be raised on bad password, allowing the program to react to this corner case?

The RFC says we can get back a "ERR_NEEDMOREPARAMS" when we send a "PASS" command but I don't think this is going to help here. 

Any idea, or am I missing something?

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/irc/issue/23
- Originally reported by: Anonymous
- Originally created at: 2013-06-04T03:43:54.239

issue_title: Change URLs for security object
issue_body:
The goal is not to depend on the app object anymore (the URLs shouldn't change anytime).

This will also give the ability to create EsiSecurity object without having to get a swagger spec before. 

- [ ] Change oauth/verify to use `https://esi.tech.ccp.is/verify` instead of `https://login.eveonline.com/oauth/verify`
- [ ] Add the ability to override (in args) the other base URLs (`https://login.eveonline.com/`) 

Keep `app` for backward compatibility, but also for the ease of using URLs, especially if someone switch between servers (TQ/SISI) they'd not need to switch URL manually, but only the swagger spec datasource.

Priority: 
- if nothing provided: TQ oauth/token
- if one given, use it
- if both (url, app), always use app

issue_title: Data <@filename isn't relative to the YAML file
issue_body:
The [docs say](https://gabbi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/format.html#data):

> If the value is a string that begins with <@ then the rest of the string is treated as the name of a file to be loaded from the same directory as the YAML file. 

But I haven't found it works like this unless I cd into the directory containing the yaml file.

`_load_data_file` says:
```python
path = os.path.join(self.test_directory, os.path.basename(filename))
```
this does a few things:
- makes the path begin with `self.test_directory` (defaults to `.`, the current working directory)
- disguards any folders in the <@ `filename` path
- appends `filename`

This means, if I'm in `/`, and I have a test that says `data: <@cake.jpg`, I cannot run my tests as advised in the docs:
`gabbi-run -- /my/test.yaml /my/other.yaml`

`FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: './cake.jpg'`

So in our test running script, we have to start with `cd tests/`.

My preference, would be to make the path relative to the location of the yaml file. That way, I can run my tests from whatever directory.

Obviously this isn't possible when redirecting with `<` as the filename is never seen by gabbi. So I'm happy is discuss other ideas.

---

Furthermore I can't keep my test data in a child directory, unless I cd into that directory like:
```shell
cd yaml_tests/test_data
gabbi-run -- ../test_things.yaml
```

So for that reason, I'd like to allow directories like `<@test_data/filename.txt` to be included in the path.

issue_title: retry_on_no_exception
issue_body:
I am using tenacity for integration testing of networking code.

Quite frequently I have to retry on a condition n times within m seconds, where the condition may not fail a single time within that time frame.

Thus, it would helpful to have a "retry_on_no_exception" option similar to the "retry_on_exception" option.

The expected behavior of the "retry_on_no_exception" option is to ...
- ... retry if no exception occurs.
- ... stop retrying and raising whichever exception has occurred, if an exception occurred.

PS: I also posted this issue for the retrying package.

issue_title: Retrying compatibility: seconds vs. ms
issue_body:
By default seconds are used for most stop/retry predicates in tenacity right now. However in retrying ms was the default.

While I don't disagree with seconds being the base of choice here, it causes more work for anyone migrating from retrying -> tenacity.

For example migrating retrying code like [1] to tenacity requires the measurements parameters passed to be changed to account for the fact that tenacity uses seconds.

I assume this is intended correct, and folks migrating just have to deal with it?

[1] https://github.com/openstack/neutron/blob/master/neutron/agent/common/ovs_lib.py#L80

issue_title: "Surviving" notation backwards?
issue_body:
Hello. First let me say I'm super excited for this repo! I literally learned about mutation testing today and immediately pip installed your package.

What's confusing me is what you call a "surviving mutant." My expectation was that surviving mutants would be those that still pass the test suite. However, [line 388](https://github.com/boxed/mutmut/blob/c86bde07edaa5340c57b4bb639e7c2f6b7c80a46/mutmut/__main__.py#L388) defines surviving mutants as those that do *not* pass the test suite. This means mutations that aren't a problem wind up being reported as "FAILED," and I haven't found a way to show the mutations that have left the test in a passing state.

TL;DR I want to know which mutants passed my test suite, not which ones don't pass my test suite.

My proposal is to remove `not` from line 388, but this drastically changes the behavior of this code base. Like I said, I'm a mutation testing noob, so it's entirely possible I'm thinking about this the wrong way.

Thanks again for the awesome repo!

issue_title: append quantities
issue_body:
Say I have some quantities that I want to put into one array. Without pint and without units, 
I would do something like:
```
array = np.array([_instance.c0 for _instance in instances])
```
However, with Pint this will give 
```
[<Quantity(0.0, 'gram')>, <Quantity(0.0, 'gram')>, <Quantity(0.0, 'gram ')>]
```
while I would like to have something like
```
<Quantity([ 0.  0.  0.], 'gram')>
```
Is there an option in Pint I am missing? I fixed this with some function and the @wraps decorator:
```
    @ureg.wraps('=A',(None, '=A', '=A'))
    def myAppend(self, array, value):
        array_out = np.append(array, value)
        return array_out

        c0 = np.array([]) * ureg('mg/L')
        for _i in instances:
            c0 = self.myAppend(c0, _i.c0)
```
I think it would be nice if the np.append would exhibit this behaviour.

issue_title: More sensible prefix + unit combinations
issue_body:
As it is now, pint is pretty liberal about combining prefixes + units.  For example, "pinch" is parsed as a "picoinch", which doesn't seem very useful to me.  Does anyone have any thoughts on supporting more fine-grained prefix + unit name combinations?  It would be helpful when parsing unverified input to have more restrictive unit names.  We could allow prefix combinations to be specified in the definitions file, so people could write their own rules.

Possible rules:
1) SI prefixes only with SI units (no "pinch" like above)
2) binary prefixes only with information units ("kibibyte" is useful, "kibimeter" less so)
3) abbreviated prefixes only with abbreviated units (allow "km" but no "kmeter")
4) no prefixes when a unit name has one built in (disallow "kilokph")

Comments?  Dissenting opinions?

issue_title: Change project name?
issue_body:
The name `pyeda` is a little more crowded than I would like:

1. `pyeda.org` points to https://code.google.com/p/pyeda
2. [Cogenda PyEDA](https://github.com/cogenda/pyEDA)
3. PyEDA also stands for Python Event Driven Architecture

I don't currently have a good idea for a new name, but I prefer names that don't have `py` in them. For example, `matplotlib`, `pandas`, `requests` and `networkx` are good. There are lots of popular libraries such as `sympy`, `numpy`, `scipy`, etc, but I feel like this type of name should be reserved for the elite few, b/c otherwise it gets repetitive. You would have py-everything and everything-py. And then there's `pypy` :).

Some useful keywords: boolean, digital, logic, bit, vlsi.

Popular culture that deals with logic:

* Alice in Wonderland
* Monty Python Holy Grail scene: "she's a witch".

Suggestions welcome, but I really have no idea if and when to do this.

issue_title: pyang batch script

While using in windows, If the user made .py always opens with text editor pyang won't detect from cmd. It always gives below message to user.

**'pyang' is not recognized as an internal or external command,
operable program or batch file.**

To avoid such cases make place %PYTHONPATH%\Scripts\pyang.bat file put below commands, pyang will work windows normally.

``` bat
@echo off
python %PYTHONPATH%\Scripts\pyang %*
```

I felt this file creation will be useful pyang users while installing pyang itself

issue_title: test_copy(self) is wrong?
issue_body:
`test_copy` appears to be a copy of the deep copy test, and not correct

        a = Dict()
        a.child.immutable = 42
        a.child.mutable = foo

        b = a.copy()

        # immutable object should not change
        b.child.immutable = 21
        self.assertEqual(a.child.immutable, 42)

the last line should read 

        self.assertEqual(a.child.immutable, 21)

The `copy()` should be a shallow copy with `b.child` pointing to the same object as `a.child`, so have been changed to 21.

issue_title: Remote DNS resolution bug when using create_connection
issue_body:
The call to sock.set_proxy inside create_connection fails to correctly pass the rdns parameter. The result is that remote dns resolution is not performed.

A workaround is to set the username to True (because the username is sent where rdns should be)

    if proxy_type is not None:
        sock.set_proxy(proxy_type, proxy_addr, proxy_port,
                       proxy_username, proxy_password)

should be

    if proxy_type is not None:
        sock.set_proxy(proxy_type, proxy_addr, proxy_port, rdns
                       proxy_username, proxy_password)

issue_title: Documentation: How fast is "fast"?
issue_body:
When a library claims to be "fast", I immediately look for benchmarks, especially those that compare the library against other Python KML libraries.

It would be a good idea to add benchmarks, similar to the way that libraries like [udatetime](https://github.com/freach/udatetime) and [fastavro](https://github.com/tebeka/fastavro) do.

issue_title: QImage to array2d<rgb_pixel>
issue_body:
Hello
Using Qt, I want to convert a QImage, having the format QImage::Format_RGB888 to array2d<rgb_pixel>.
After some googling, I came up with
void qImage2array2d(const QImage &src, dlib::array2d<dlib::rgb_pixel> &dst)
{
    dlib::assign_image(dst,
                       dlib::mat((const dlib::rgb_pixel *)src.bits(),
                                 src.height(), src.width() /*, src.bytesPerLine()/3 */));
}
It works well when src.bytesPerLine() is 3 * src.width(). But the stride parameter seems not to be in bytes.
Therefore, I find no way to compensate the padding used by QImage to make all rows to be 32 bit aligned.
Is there any better way to do this in dlib except for using memcpy row by row to build a data buffer without padding?

issue_title: shape_predictor Segmentation fault.
issue_body:
I worked on an ordinary computer running debian 9 and everything was fine. Face recognition worked perfectly.

There was a need to compile this application under arm on Raspbian.
After a long, but successful compilation (without errors) I got the application.
At startup and debugging it was found out that the application comes up in execution before

issue_title: I can't import the module installed with pip.
issue_body: Title. Or it takes an unreasonable amount of time. Either way it's not useable as it is.

issue_title: Metadata from metadata processors gets stored in wrong place
issue_body: Currently, the hierarchy of metadata is duplicated: if an asset contains EXIF and IPTC, it will be stored as {'exif': {'exif': ..., 'iptc': ...}}

issue_title: Remove dependencies between Processors
issue_body: Currently, `PillowProcessor` depends on `PiexifProcessor` to extract Exif metadata from the asset essence. This task should be performed in madam.read instead.

issue_title: How can we improve the experience on Linux?
issue_body:
Presently there are a few large user experience issues with Keyring on Linux:

1. The dependency on python-dbus is tricky. It seems like moving to Jeepney is already being considered: https://github.com/jaraco/keyring/issues/301
2. Working with headless installations is [difficult](https://github.com/jaraco/keyring#using-keyring-on-headless-linux-systems). Would it be possible for us to consider starting the dbus daemon automatically if needed?

issue_title: "Could not deserialize key data"
issue_body:
Hi.  I'm receiving a "Could not deserialize key data" error in the following code.  What am I doing wrong?  I have investigated high/low trying to figure out if this public_key is in PEM format and it appears as if it is but I'm not sure (how can I verify this?).  The decoding (and encoding also) works in the jwt.io debugger.  Also, I traced through the pyjwt code and it's bombing during the verification of the signature.  If I specify "verify=False" then the payload is returned just fine.  I'm using the latest version of all packages.  Thanks for the help.

UPDATE:  I got this to work by adding line separators in the public key as follows:
- Right after the begin public key:  -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n
- And another one right before the end public key:  \n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

I figured this out after I dove into the openssl library a little.  The following command failed with an "Error: offset too large" after I simply copied and pasted the entire string into my public.key file:
`openssl asn1parse -inform PEM -i -in public.key`
After adding the line separators into the file then it worked.
```
import jwt

payload = {
    "sub": "1234567890",
    "name": "John Doe",
    "iss": "xena princess warrior",
    "aud": "very cool aud"
}

private_key = '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY----------END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----'

token = 'eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiaXNzIjoieGVuYSBwcmluY2VzcyB3YXJyaW9yIiwiYXVkIjoidmVyeSBjb29sIGF1ZCJ9.KHQbQ_FSUKISBVM7WbESA5k2r3D1W02VKnmiWpIvyAST4ysP_1zRH2fd-f1_Sb4uagI9u7PiFQqtPZcLflLjLqH5I1rzjb21FTfdL2nR2v8Ra3r-6AcbjFTgdmjrf9qAZ-Ii689wP1-hjJeg8gCZmg0lkyEcMtECopFaqd5jUxY'
```

issue_title: Allow a list of valid audiences to be configured
issue_body:
A JWT token can contain multiple audiences.

But, it would be nice if you could also specify a list of valid audiences for `audience` in `jwt.decode()`.

Example:

``` Python
options = {
	'verify_signature': True,
	'verify_exp': True,
	'verify_nbf': True,
	'verify_iat': True,
	'verify_aud': True,
	'verify_iss': True,
	'require_exp': False,
	'require_iat': False,
	'require_nbf': False
}

payload = jwt.decode(
	jwt_value,
	key=self._public_key,
	verify=True,
	audience=["audience_1", "audience_2"],
	issuer="SomeIssuer",
	options=options,
)
```

issue_title: datawriter creation wastes a serial model run
issue_body:
When the datawriter is created, a full model run is needed, just to get the size of the simulation. This behaviour is fine for fast models (run time<1 min) but for slow models, this is not acceptable. I see two options:
1. Use the evaluation to get the dimensions
1. A new parameter for the sampler where the user can give the size of the simulation (optional, if not given the model run is performed and the results are used for the dimension)

Any thoughts?

issue_title: TypeError while retrieving ProvDocuments from ProvStore using Py3
issue_body:
Hello,

I tried out the Tutorial given at http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/trungdong/notebooks/blob/master/PROV%20Tutorial.ipynb, everything very nice explained. But I always got exceptions when trying to retrieve ProvDocuments from ProvStore looking like this:

TypeError: initial_value must be str or None, not bytes

This exception was raised by the deserializer from model.py because the content it tried to parse were bytes. I found the origin of the problem in the request call at line 106 r= self._request(...), in api.py from provstore-api module, which returns the content as bytes and that raised the error from the io.StringIO function in the deserializer which accepts only unicode. I simply decoded the content as UTF-8, right before the deserializer is called from the api and everything worked again. I don't know what is responsible for this incompatibility, maybe Windows 7 which I'm using? Anyway please check the compatibility of these modules again.

Best wishes
Bojan

issue_title: min_child_node_size defaults to None
issue_body: min_child_node_size defaults to None so that it doesn't break current API at version 2. This is inconsistent with min_parent_node_size and will thus have to be changed come version 3

issue_title: Weekday should support 0-7 range; 7 being Sunday.
issue_body:
The `weekday` field should be support numeric range `0-7` in order to support zero and one based inputs.

Normally, the inputs are `0-6` with Sunday being zero and Saturday being six. However, Sunday being seven is also a valid possibility.

issue_title: Why do polygons have dtype('O') rather than something numeric?
issue_body:
When using `gds_read`, I found that the polygons are represented as `numpy.ndarray` of `dtype('O')`, i.e. `object`, not, say `float` or 'float64'.

For feeding the polygons to programs downstream, sometimes this matters, sometimes not.  For example [matplotlib](http://matplotlib.org) and [FreeCAD](http://www.freecadweb.org) recognize the coordinates as numbers but [PyGmsh](https://github.com/nschloe/pygmsh) doesn't.

To demonstrate, take any gds, say tutorial.gds from here, and 

    gds = GdsLibrary()
    gds.read_gds(argv[1])

    for layer, v in gds.top_level()[0].get_polygons(True).iteritems():
        print(layer, map(lambda p: (p.shape, p.dtype), v))

The workaround is trivial of course, just `p.astype(float)` (as in `Polygon.fillet`) or `np.asarray(p, dtype=float)` if a function is preferred to a method, but I was curious as to why the coordinates weren't numeric in the first place.

issue_title: Only fix E128 if it does not cause E501
issue_body:
`autopep8 --select E128` fixes this one:

             if widget_js_attributes is not None:
                 raise PendingDeprecationWarning('widget_js_attributes are '
    -                    'deprecated in favor of widget_attrs')
    +                                            'deprecated in favor of widget_attrs')

But then it causes E501 (line too long).

It would be useful if the E128 wasn't fixed in this case.

The real / best fix is to change it to the following instead, which would
require to join the strings:

             if widget_js_attributes is not None:
                 raise PendingDeprecationWarning(
                     'widget_js_attributes are deprecated in favor of widget_attrs')

Optionally maybe also:

                 raise PendingDeprecationWarning(
                     'widget_js_attributes are '
                     'deprecated in favor of widget_attrs')

issue_title: Send() method used without checking that all data was sent
issue_body:
The [send()](http://paramiko-docs.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/channel.html#paramiko.channel.Channel.send) method of Paramiko channels doesn't guarantee that all the data gets sent, but there is a [sendall()](http://paramiko-docs.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/channel.html#paramiko.channel.Channel.sendall) method that does. Using sendall() saves you from having to write your own loop.

This is an easy swap - I'll submit a PR for review.

issue_title: Timeout using ssh config
issue_body:
There is, what I would consider, a bug in the way ssh config files are used to proxy.

Normally your ssh config allows you to do this:
```
Host *
    ServerAliveInterval 5

Host *.domain
    ProxyCommand ssh -W %h:%p domain_proxy

Host my_host.domain
    HostName 10.10.10.10
```
when you ssh to my_host.domain, it will apply the ServerAliveInterval from Host *, the ProxyCommand from *.domain, and the IP address from my_Host.domain.
like so:
```bash
> ssh -vvvv my_host.domain
OpenSSH_7.2p2 Ubuntu-4ubuntu1, OpenSSL 1.0.2g-fips  1 Mar 2016
debug1: Reading configuration data ssh_config
debug1: ssh_config line 1: Applying options for *
debug1: ssh_config line 17: Applying options for *.domain
debug1: ssh_config line 45: Applying options for my_host.domain*
debug1: Executing proxy command: exec ssh -W 10.10.10.10:22 domain_proxy
...
```
Netmiko, on the other hand, will not find the ProxyCommand under *.domain, you must set all of the options under the Host its self like so:
```
Host my_host.domain
    HostName 10.10.10.10
    ProxyCommand ssh -W %h:%p domain_proxy
    ServerAliveInterval 5
```
If you must use a proxy to connect, the first ssh config, without ProxyCommand specified under the host, will give this error:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/lbernard/miniconda3/envs/netmiko/lib/python2.7/site-packages/netmiko/ssh_dispatcher.py", line 84, in ConnectHandler
    return ConnectionClass(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/lbernard/miniconda3/envs/netmiko/lib/python2.7/site-packages/netmiko/base_connection.py", line 68, in __init__
    self.establish_connection(verbose=verbose, use_keys=use_keys, key_file=key_file)
  File "/home/lbernard/miniconda3/envs/netmiko/lib/python2.7/site-packages/netmiko/base_connection.py", line 169, in establish_connection
    raise NetMikoTimeoutException(msg)
netmiko.ssh_exception.NetMikoTimeoutException: Connection to device timed-out: cisco_ios my_host.domain:22
```

issue_title: Pass variables from high level graph to low-level level one
issue_body:
If low-level graph field is accepting options for entity filter, there should a possibility to pass the variables down from top level graph. This will allow to avoid unnecessary query and optimize db queries.
Example:
High level graph:
```
Field('regionName', String, company_sg.c(
            get_company_region(
                S.this.address,
                each(S.branch, S.this.branches, S.branch.address.region_id),
                S.regionId
            )
        ), options=[Option('regionId', Optional[Integer], default=None)]),
```
This field uses region as a way to filter branches on the client side. If we could pass the variable, we could filter them server side limiting the amount of entities we need to process to zero in most common cases

```
Field('regionName', String, company_sg.c(
            get_company_region(
                S.this.address,
                each(S.branch, S.this.branches(S.regionId), S.branch.address.region_id),
                S.regionId
            )
        ), options=[Option('regionId', Optional[Integer], default=None)]),
```

```python
from docker import Client

)
pty = PseudoTerminal(client, operation)
```

```python
start(client, container, interactive=True, logs=False)
```

I haven't yet investigated any further.

issue_title: Provide extended net_io_counters() metrics
issue_body: The same way we added extended platform specific disk IO metrics on Linux and FreeBSD (busy_time and merged r/w times, see https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/pull/523 and https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/issues/756) we should do the same for net_io_counters(). There's a bunch of metrics like error packets on Linux which we are currently ignoring and not returning just for the sake of cross-platform conformity.

issue_title: Make Proc Directory Injectable
issue_body:
I'm writing a Docker container for a plugin service that will be able to read the resource stats from the Docker host. In the container, I have to pass the host's proc directory as `/host/proc` instead of slapping it over the container's `/proc`. This requirement renders this library useless for reading the host's proc info, since it assumes where proc is located on a linux machine. If I could inject the read path into your utility, I wouldn't have to write a custom parser for this project. 

Either a setting like `psutil.proc_path = "/host/proc"` or an object with the path initializer, `util = PsUtil("/host/proc")` would be awesome to have.

```python
import pytz
```

```python
import pytz

```

Is it normal?

issue_title: Suggestion: namedtuples for entries
issue_body:
Thanks for creating this library. I've a suggestion for improvement: use named tuples for entries rather than plain tuples. Here's an example:

    from collections import namedtuple
    Entry = namedtuple('Entry', ['start', 'end', 'label'])
    
    # Example
    entry = Entry(0.0, 0.30, 'sound')
    print(entry)

Result: `Entry(start=0.0, end=0.3, label='sound')`

The nice thing about this is that you don't have to do any indexing anymore, but you can just do `entry.start` to get the starting time.

issue_title: discord.colour has poor data validation
issue_body:
I don't know if this is a bug or status by design but I was trying to randomize embed colours and was passing string based hex values to `discord.colour`

    listing_embed = discord.Embed(title=self.title, description=self.description,
                                                        color=discord.Colour(hex(random.randint(0, 16777215))),
                                                        url=self.url)

I had assumed that this was working fine since there was not error thrown at declaration. However using that code netted me a HTTP 400 error on submission. I have since changed it to 

     listing_embed = discord.Embed(title=self.title, description=self.description,
                                                        color=discord.Colour(random.randint(0, 16777215)),
                                                        url=self.url)

Which works exactly how I wanted it to. I would have done that in the first place if the data I passed it was rejected. I was reading http://discordpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html?highlight=colour#discord.Embed.colour which is why I thought about using `.Colour()`. FYI I am not great at Python but looking at colour.py I noticed this for the constructor

    def __init__(self, value):
        self.value = value

Yes, the docs for Colour support that it should be int. Should there not be some data validation there or no? I am told, in #help, that this is not an issue in the rewrite branch.

issue_title: Cronjob shell code should move to Python (safety/security)
issue_body:
Shouldn't the cronjob shell script go into the Python code base? The syntax could be something like this:

```
python acme_tiny.py --renew-cert ...
```

Existing issues such as #112 and #117 show that the cronjob shell script is not optimal and needs to be fixed. Do we really want to maintain these safety improvements in the README?

I understand that the code base should stay small, but the renewal is a vital part of the system. Moving that out into a user-managed shell script means that any code audit will be incomplete. So it should be maintained in the Python code base which is easily updated, rather than in the README where some old version may have been copied into the system and never be updated again. Also, proper error handling is much easier in Python than in a shell script.

issue_title: How to best signal aliveness
issue_body:
I have a situation where I have two consumers (c1 and c2), and want to poll from c1 until there is nothing left (c1.poll(..) returns empty), and then start polling from c2 (and check c1 again afterwards). Essentially, c1 has priority over c2.

Now, this means I'll be polling frequently from c1, and ideally I just want to keep c2 alive until I need it. If I don't poll it at all until I want to, I eventually get kafka.error.CommitFailedError when I commit c2, because the time between its polls was too long and things have rebalanced.

So I tried interleaving c1.poll(...) with something like c2.poll(timeout, 0), just to signal that it  shouldn't rebalance, and I don't want records right now, but this runs into the assertion that the max_records needs to be larger than 0.

So is there any way to signal (a heartbeat of sorts) that c2 is still there, I just don't want any records from it right now? Something poll(timeout, 0) would achieve, I guess, if it was allowed.

Thanks!
-Joris.

issue_title: MIDDLEWARE order
issue_body:
The docs say that `WhiteNoiseMiddleware` should come after Django's `SecurityMiddleware`, but don't refer to any or Django's other middlewares. So *maybe* it doesn't matter, but some other middlewares have specific instructions and I'm not sure if they're relevant.

For example `django.middleware.cache.UpdateCacheMiddleware` "must be before those that modify the `Vary` header" - does Whitenoise do that?

And `django.middleware.gzip.GZipMiddleware` must go "before any middleware that may change or use the response body". Again, is that Whitenoise?

Apologies for my ignorance, but without delving into the gritty details of how these things work I'm not sure if I'm setting things up correctly.

issue_title: Cannot import exceptions
issue_body: The `__all__` list in `__init__.py` does not include the exception classes. They cannot therefore be imported and caught.

issue_title: Feature request: Temp holds?
issue_body: Maybe you can use some of the research from here. https://github.com/texnofobix/pyhwtherm/tree/2.0-dev :)

issue_title: README.md references undesirable Arch package `yaourt`
issue_body:
Very nitpicky, but I'd very much prefer `README.md` would reference a different AUR helper than `yaourt`, since it is pretty much ubiquitously considered one of the worst AUR helpers of today.

Looking at the [Comparison Table](https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/AUR_helpers#Comparison_table), I would suggest replacing the `yaourt` reference with something such as `pacaur` (which uses the same syntax as `pacman`, as does `yaourt`, simplifying the modification in `README.md` to a single word substitution.

issue_title: Grafana Dashboard outdated for InfluxDB 0.11.x
issue_body:
Potentially relates to  #648.

#### Description

I recently updated InfluxDB from 0.9.x to 0.11.1 and updated my InfluxDB databases as per [https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v0.10/administration/upgrading/](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v0.10/administration/upgrading/) and [https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v0.11/administration/upgrading/](https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v0.11/administration/upgrading/).

I used the output of the
`$ influx config`
command to use as my influxdb.conf, and then appended the following to it to setup InfluxDB:
[influxdb]
  host = "localhost"
  port = "8086"
  user = "root"
  password = "root"
  db = "glances"
  prefix = "localhost"

Grafana shows "No datapoints" in each of my graphs.  I noticed that glances/conf/glances-grafana.json hasn't been updated since Dec 15, 2015.  Perhaps this is the issue?

#### Versions

* Glances (glances -V): 2.6.1
* PSutil (glances -V): 4.1.0
* Operating System (lsb_release -a): Ubuntu 14.04.4 LTS

#### Logs

Excerpt from /var/log/influxdb/influxd.log:
[http] 2016/04/01 13:48:48 127.0.0.1 - root [01/Apr/2016:13:48:48 -0400] GET /query?db=glances&epoch=ms&q=SELECT+mean%28%22min1%22%29+FROM+%22localhost.load%22+WHERE+time+%3E+now%28%29+-+6h+GROUP+BY+time%2830s%29+fill%28null%29%0ASELECT+mean%28%22min5%22%29+FROM+%22localhost.load%22+WHERE+time+%3E+now%28%29+-+6h+GROUP+BY+time%2830s%29+fill%28null%29%0ASELECT+mean%28%22min15%22%29+FROM+%22localhost.load%22+WHERE+time+%3E+now%28%29+-+6h+GROUP+BY+time%2830s%29+fill%28null%29 HTTP/1.1 200 92 https://IP-address/dashboard/file/glances-grafana.json?panelId=4&fullscreen&edit Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_11_4) AppleWebKit/601.5.17 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/9.1 Safari/601.5.17 fd172c71-f831-11e5-80c7-000000000000 1.49804ms

Based on this, the queries seem to be working, but I think the queries may have to be changed.  I found this doc, which outlines API changes from 0.10.x to 0.11.x:
https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v0.11/concepts/010_vs_011/#breaking-api-changes

The datasource in Grafana is also set to InfluxDB 0.9.x, which is said to work with 0.10.x, but I'm not sure about 0.11.x.  This may also be the issue.

Thanks!

issue_title: False negative on response status code
issue_body:
Settings:
1. `flex` is asked to load a `YAML` file
2. `flex` reports that the `status_code` `'200'` is not part of dictionary keys: `200, 400, 404`.

The bug is really because `flex` converts the `status_code` to `str`, but when it loads the `responses` from the schema, it never converted the keys to `str` (`YAML` parser by default treats these as integers). Hence, the false negative.

I'll probably submit a pull request later, and here is the cute animals "picture": 🐰 😸

Oddly enough I am receiving the 'foo' alias for '123'.  However, I can force the my_name alias by using a function:

This is the function I'm using to fetch from the cursor into a dictionary:

```
def db_extract(cnn, file, patient):
    with open(file) as f:
        sql = f.read()

    events = []
    csr = cnn.cursor()

```

issue_title: Remove 'interruptable' option.
issue_body:
Previously, one always had to deal with InterruptError, as is done in private method ``_char_is_ready`` (previously, public method ``kbhit``).

Beginning in python3.5, interruptions are automatically retried, and such code is not required any longer.

We should follow suite and automatically retry, and remove the toggle option to disable this behavior to match python3.5.  I can't think of any reason why somebody might want ``keystroke`` (previously, ``inkey``) to return earlier than ``timeout`` specified when no keypress is received.

issue_title: Add native support for Python UUID data type
issue_body:
Looking through the Wiki documentation on [Data Types](https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc/wiki/Data-Types/de8c706abd83214d69e6be0b5c2bb58b2c916212), I noticed that the SQL_GUID type is being converted to/from a string, instead of using [the native `UUID` type](https://docs.python.org/2/library/uuid.html) provided by Python. The SQL_GUID type applies to the `uniqueidentifer` column type, which [pyodbc appears to be testing](https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc/blob/f94251a5b62252fff0e7057b6f70260cb4bf4fb0/tests2/accesstests.py#L426-L435) against.

These tests don't appear to have changed since 2008 (added in https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc/commit/c3f6b46d8a4f73722439b4755ef5df42adff81d9), and there is a comment for the test that alludes to Python not having a UUID type. This was probably true back in 2008, but Python has come a long way since then and now it does have a UUID type.

issue_title: Support async generator for <Any>StreamMethod classes
issue_body:
The current implementation of the UnaryStreamMethod assumes that it will accumulate all replies before returning. This is not always desirable.

For example, in my use case I have a Unary_Stream method that provides a publisher style interface. The client sends a Unary request to open the channel and then expects to process a never ending stream of updates. In this use case I need to be able to receive and process individual updates as they are received. In this use case an async generator style interface would be preferable.

Something along the lines of:
```python
async for update in stub.UnaryStreamGreeting(request):
    handle_update(update)
```

I can kind of achieve what I want if I modify the ``grpclib.client.py`` code a little to be:
```python
class UnaryStreamMethod(ServiceMethod):

    async def __call__(self, message, *, timeout=None, metadata=None):
        async with self.open(timeout=timeout, metadata=metadata) as stream:
            await stream.send_message(message, end=True)
            # return await _to_list(stream)
            async for reply in stream.recv_message():
                yield reply
```
However this will only work with the more advanced usage such as this:
```python
    async with stub.UnaryStreamGreeting.open() as stream:
        print(f'sending {request.name}')
        await stream.send_message(request, end=True)
        async for response in stream:
            print(f'received: {response.message}')
```
and not with the simpler usage such as this:
```python
responses = await stub.UnaryStreamGreeting(request)
```
which raises:
```python
TypeError: object async_generator can't be used in 'await' expression
```

Perhaps implementing ``__aiter__`` and ``__anext__`` on the UnaryStreamMethod class could provide the appropriate API allowing it to be used as an async generator to yield individual messages.

issue_title: ThemedStyle.theme_use doesn't work without arguments
issue_body:
`ttk.Style().theme_use()` can be called with no arguments to check which ttk theme is currently being used, but `ttkthemes.ThemedStyle` doesn't support it.

```python
>>> from tkinter import ttk
>>> import ttkthemes
>>> ttk.Style().theme_use()
'default'
>>> ttkthemes.ThemedStyle().theme_use()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: set_theme() missing 1 required positional argument: 'theme_name'
>>>
```

Of course, this is easy to fix with e.g. `style.tk.eval("return $ttk::currentTheme")` (yes, I got this from tkinter's code), but it would be nice if the `theme_use` method was compatible with the `theme_use` it overrides (instead of just aliasing it to `set_theme`).

issue_title: Sending gauges with negative values
issue_body:
Hi and thanks for this project 

There is a bit of a [pitfail](https://github.com/etsy/statsd/blob/master/docs/metric_types.md#gauges) with gauges in statsd: if you send a number with a sign (any sign), statsd will add/subtract the value instead of setting it. Say, if you send a metric with "-1" over time, graphite (or whatever is that that you send your statsd metrics to) will draw a downward trend, which isn't expected/intuitive behaviour at all. The user is going to have to go and find this special case in the statsd docs. I propose that a new method will be added to statsd.Gauge to set the metric value no matter the sign. What do you think?

issue_title: "L.512+MVG" is a URL?
issue_body:
The beginning of the tweet looks like this: "Sperrung der Selbeckerstr L.512+MVG Linie 84".
The estimated character count is too high because "L.512+MVG" is seen as a URL requiring 23 characters.
This ended up giving me an error because the overall tweet length was too much.

I tried some other strings, it seems like `(any string containing an alphabetic character and not starting with a dot)+"."+(any string starting with a number)` is seen as a URL.

issue_title: chronological order under multiple data source

For each iteration on next(self) of bt.Strategy, the datetimes of the 2 data are not aligned. I understand the users have to make sure the data are aligned before adding to the cerebro, but I think the behavior is unexpected. 

Will it be a good idea to make sure that data have same datetime on each next(self) being called. If one of the data has gap, it should be None or automatically forward backfilled.

issue_title: compatibility with Python 2 💡
issue_body:
Hi, and thanks for this module.

I'm developing an API documentation tool for python, because I find Sphinx over-engineer, reSt less readable then Markdown, and using *inspect* instead of *ast* limiting.  
Anyhow, for this project I was using Python-Markdown, but I'm bothered by critics toward Markdown lack of precise specs ([some ](http://www.zverovich.net/2016/06/16/rst-vs-markdown.html) call it *not semantic*), Therefore I've been looking for a Markdown python module that implements Commonmark. Unfortunately Python-Markdown's creator is quite against it... point of view I guess.  
I also need extensions to allow people to create, for example, admonitions like in Sphinx and reSt and this made Commonmark-py not a suitable candidate for my tool.

Now mistletoe seems to have all I need with one exception: It's only for Python3.
If I really want to make a documentation tool, I can't just ignore that a lot of people (me included) are still using python2.

So my question is very simple: Are you willing to make mistletoe compatible with both python2 and python3? Would you accept pushes to your repository to make this happen? Or are you completely against it?

I have already started this conversion. Most of the changes are really minimal.

Cheers,
   Dan

issue_title: Cannot replace with multiple lines
issue_body:
It seems that bumpversion doesn't work with multiline string.

Here is my `CHANGELOG.md`:

```
## Unreleased ##

  - xxxx

## 0.0.2 -- 2015-12-08 ##

  - yyyy
```

And here is my `.bumpversion.cfg`:

```
[bumpversion:file:CHANGELOG.md]
search = ## Unreleased ##
replace = ## Unreleased ##
  
  ## {new_version} - {utcnow:%Y-%m-%d} ##
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After running bumpversion, the *expected* changelog is:

```
## Unreleased ##

## 0.0.3 -- 2015-12-09 ##

  - xxxx

## 0.0.2 -- 2015-12-08 ##

  - yyyy
```

But the *actual* content is:

```
## Unreleased ##
## 0.0.3 -- 2015-12-09 ##

  - xxxx

## 0.0.2 -- 2015-12-08 ##

  - yyyy
```

And `.bumpversion.cfg` is changed:

```
[bumpversion:file:CHANGELOG.md]
search = ## Unreleased ##
replace = ## Unreleased ##
        ## {new_version} - {utcnow:%Y-%m-%d} ##
```

Bumpversion removes the empty line.



How to replace current version with multiline string?

issue_title: Are transits correctly calculated for eccentric orbits?
issue_body:
Dear PyAstronomy Team,
Thanks for your excellent package. I'm using now for a port of an old code, and it's really coming in handy.

I have a question about the way in which transits are calculated when the orbit is allowed to be eccentric in PyAstronomy version 0.13.0b0. Inside the modelSuite/XTran/forTrans directory in the mandelAgol.py file on line 143, I see the following: 
```
    if orbit == "keplerian":
      self["w"] = -90.0
```

If I'm reading the documentation correctly, that line is setting the argument of periapsis to -90 degrees. Why make that assumption? If one is modeling an orbit with a different orientation, doesn't this line make the transit calculation come out wrong?

Thanks,
Brian

issue_title: Fix URL segment concatenation
issue_body:
Currently we join URL segments by string concatenation is... not great, especially combined with Unicode. 

We should use existing libraries to do this correctly: 
urlparse.urljoin

This is the urlparse library (Py2) / urllib.parse (Py3).  

Bonus: this lets us supply base URLs but also merge with user-supply query params (down the road).

This is a side element of https://github.com/svanoort/pyresttest/issues/104

issue_title: Document how to get command output as a stream
issue_body:
Sometimes the output of a command is too large to store into memory, or a process needs to be handled line by line and splitting by newlines is costly. This is a must-have for me but it wasn't listed anywhere on the website.

Currently this is achieved by
```python
plumbum.local['cat']['foo.txt'].popen().iter_lines()
```
Which returns an iterator of lists [stdout, stderr] (as a side note the documentation claims it returns tuples).

* fix tuple/list inconsistency with the docs 
* add an iterator example in the docs

Might also think about adding a version that behaves like `.run()` but returns an iterator of stdout.

issue_title: Initial calculation of unicode table is not atomic
issue_body: If you're using e.g. pytest-xdist this can result in a corrupt unicode table, which is bad. Instead the file should be created under a temporary name then moved atomically.

issue_title: UserDefinedField Initialize a value
get_chromecasts() no connection on Windows 10/Server 2016
issue_body:
Greetings,

So, I'm running into a rather big issue in creating a plugin for Plex Media Server to be able to interact with cast devices headlessly. 

On Linux, my implementation works perfectly. Cast devices are discovered and cached, and I'm about to get ready to work on a playback implementation.

However, on Windows, it fails to find any devices - both inside my Plex Plugin implementation, as well as directly through the python console using pip imports.

Originally, I had some virtual adapters in play for Hyper-V, but even after removing them, verifying WIndows Firewall was off, etc. - it doesn't connect. Specifically, in the py console, I'm seeing "pychromecast.socket_client:Failed to connect, retrying in 5.0s" on trying to discover devices. In the Plex plugin, it just doesn't find anything.

What's interesting is that in Plex, if I wireshark my connection for UDP on 5353, I can see both the outgoing query form the server, as well as the responses from my cast devices on the LAN. However, it seems like pyChromeCast is not binding properly to the socket to listen for responses?

I'm no expert in any of this stuff, but I do have a PHP app that does cast discovery, and I recall having to work out a similar issue between Linux and windows where setting the SO_REUSEADDR or SO_REUSEPORT flags in the right sequence was necessary in order to get Windows discovering.

However, that didn't work here, and as I said, I'm at a loss. I'll continue poking around at things, but I'm very new to python, so I'm not as adept at messing with stuff.

Any other information I can provide - just let me know. Plex uses python 2.7 for it's core, I believe, and the console version I'm testing with is v3...

Also tested on my laptop running windows 10, it also failed to discover any devices in Plex - and discovery takes a looooong time before returning an empty array.

issue_title: pychromecast.error.MultipleChromecastsFoundError: More than one Chromecast was found specifying the filter criteria: {}
issue_body:
I am getting some troubles when having more than one cast device online and using `pychromecast.get_chromecast()` function as shown below:

```
self.cast = pychromecast.get_chromecast(self.castto)
```

The error being:

```
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pychromecast/__init__.py", line 168, in get_chromecast
    'the filter criteria: {}'.format(filters))
pychromecast.error.MultipleChromecastsFoundError: More than one Chromecast was found specifying the filter criteria: {}
```

The complete code can be seen here: https://github.com/muammar/mkchromecast/blob/master/mkchromecast/cast.py

I would be glad if someone could shed a light here because I own only one cast device. Thanks.

issue_title: Unable to Detect Paused / Idle
issue_body:
Hi, I added some triggers to try to detect state transitions on the chromecast from playing to idle OR playing to paused.  I started noticing that the triggers never occurred.  Upon examining the chromecast states themselves, it appeared that I could only see off and playing most of the time, with a brief idle when transitioning as follows off -> idle -> playing.

However, in all other cases, paused and idle cannot be detected. 
I.e.
playing -> idle
playing -> paused
Note: Using hassio to pause DOES work.  But, using, say Netflix, to pause doesn't work.

paused -> playing DOES work.  But, it's only if hassio is used to pause, and either of netflix or hassio is used to play.

I suspect some of this just wasn't planned for implementation, but I'm not sure.  Any understanding/idea would be helpful.  Perhaps I can try to learn a little to see if I can help as well.  I admit I am a complete Python novice though.

issue_title: Form defaults overwrite model data
issue_body:
Hey,
first of all, this is a great library, saved me a ton of time, thanks a lot!

I noticed a weird quirk that has been bugging me for quite some time - sometimes when I get a template with djangular form and simultaneously get model data for the form, defaults from the form overwrite the model. For example:
 
- I have a field in my django model `name = models.CharField(default="Placeholder")`
- go to a page where I edit the model with template of the rendered djangular form
- in the controller get the model through angular resource
- the name input box shows "Placeholder" instead of the real value and when I log the model, the name attribute is overwritten

When I have the template cached this doesn't happen, so I suspect this only happens when the template gets downloaded after the actual model. Also when I comment out `restoreInputField` from the `ngModel` directive in django-angular.js, this does not happen anymore (but then the defaults stop working in blank forms).

My hacky solution is to copy the model and after a timeout set it back (dealing with the time difference of arrival between the model and the form template), but this quickly gets out of hand. I guess the proper fix would be to check if the model isn't set to anything during restoring the fields. I would submit a PR, but I'm not sure if this is the right way to fix it or if I'm not doing this entirely wrong.

Thanks!

Similarly to `tablib` (see kennethreitz/tablib#329) `records` project could benefit from additional maintainer.

Facts:

- apart from that, nobody seems to be assigned as a maintainer
- commits:
  - @chadwhitacre has 11 commits
  - @hrldcpr  and @mcdallas have 6/5 commits
  - @Tethik, @gdtroszak, @Brobin have 3 commits

  - 18 other contributors with 1 commit
- Issues: 47 closed, 34 open
  - 17 open issues younger than 12 month

  - all are fresh (created by me)

issue_title: SVO Logic Adapter
issue_body:
It turns out I made a minor mistake going with Pattern. Since it is not available on Python 3.x yet, we will need to change the logic adapter to use another SVO triplet identifier. We can use nlpnet, but that contains a fairly large dependency file which I am not sure is worth it. If that doesn't work, we could use a simple SVO triplet identifier I created using NLTK's built-in POS Tagger, but I am not confident in its ability to correctly identify the subject, verb, and object of complex sentences.

@gunthercox what course of action would you prefer?

- If the tables do not exist, then they should be created.
- The `create=True` parameter should be removed.

```

example: "{% if conditional %} It was true. {% else %} It was false. {% endif %} That's all!"
```

issue_title: custom_init option
issue_body:
Init-only variables and `__post_init__ ` can be replaced with `custom_init` option which cause dataclass to generate ``__init__`` method with some different name like `__dataclass_init__` and this method can be used from `__init__`, an example:
```python
@dataclass(custom_init=True)
class C:
    a: float
    b: float
    c: float = field(init=False)

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        self.__dataclass_init__(args, kwargs) # < generated init method
        self.c = kwargs['a'] + kwargs['b'] # < custom init code
```
As an result we have custom init method, without overhead for `__post_init__ ` and `InitVar `, this pattern much simpler

issue_title: & incorrectly escaped.
issue_body:
`latexcodec` incorrectly escapes ampersands. Consider a string with latex directives like this
```
import codecs
import latexcodec

string = '\\& \\"a \\"o \\"u'
latex = codecs.encode(string, 'latex')
for byte in latex:
    print('{:>3}: {:>2}'.format(byte, codecs.decode(bytes([byte]), 'utf8')))
```
This will output
```
 92:  \
 92:  \
 38:  &
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
 97:  a
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
111:  o
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
117:  u
```
As can be seen, while the backslashes for `äüö`  are not duplicated, `latexenc` does not seem to realise that `\\&` is an escape sequence and thus prefixes `&` with another backslash, which need to be escaped in the python string as well.

issue_title: Encoding: Delimiting macros using space is wrong in conjunction with bibtex
issue_body:
In BibTeX bibliography files, spaces in author names get translated into non-breaking spaces (`~`). 
E.g., when used to encode author names, the following happens:
Source -> encoded -> after bibtex
`Yılmaz` -> `Y\i lmaz` -> `Y\i~maz`
`İmamoğlu` -> `\.Imamo\u glu` -> `\.Imamo\u~glu`
Instead we should get `Y\i{}lmaz` and  `\.Imamo\u{g}lu` or `\.Imamo\u{}glu`.
I was able to fix the first by patching the encoder method `get_space_bytes` such that `{}` is used when a macro needs to be separated. The second one however I was unable to fix, since the space is part of the replacement text in the translation table.

issue_title: Re-entrant Lock
issue_body: A follow-up to #89 . Using MariaDB's `metadata_lock_info`, a re-entrant lock, like `threading.RLock`, could be implemented, by querying `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.metadata_lock_info` each time to determine if this is the first enter on the lock or not. Would need to be thread safe.

issue_title: choice of returned histogram datatype
issue_body:
Thanks for writing this fast histogram package. A task that we astronomers tend to do often and repeatedly :) 

Coming back to the package itself, it seems to me that the return datatype of the histograms are `doubles`. Conceptually, the histogram is simply a count in that bin, and should be of an integral type. Is the return datatype chosen to be `double` to reflect the input array type? Or is that in keeping with the `numpy` convention?

Changing the histogram increment to be an integral type (i.e., the postfix `++` operator) might also lead to better performance. (There are some opportunity for performance improvements -- e.g., pointer operations, reduction of scope for variables, unrolled loops + separate counters)

Separately, any particular reason to perform a *bitwise and* (`&`) rather than the *logical and* (`&&`) [here](https://github.com/astrofrog/fast-histogram/blob/master/fast_histogram/_histogram_core.c#L109)?

Apparently, `ErrorServerBusy` is thrown sometimes when we need to back off on requests to the server. We should catch this error and let it the rate-limiting loop. Refs #354

Architecturally, rate-limiting is currently handled in `post_ratelimited()`. Unfortunately, this code doesn't know how to read `ErrorServerBusy` errors contained in the SOAP response, so some level of redesign is needed.

issue_title: Include sub-configuration files / allow configuration split
issue_body:
From 7.3p1 and up, ssh supports the `Include` option for `~/.ssh/config`: 

```
 Include
             Include the specified configuration file(s).  Multiple pathnames may be specified and each pathname may contain glob(3) wildcards and, for user configurations, shell-like
             ``~'' references to user home directories.  Files without absolute paths are assumed to be in ~/.ssh if included in a user configuration file or /etc/ssh if included from the
             system configuration file.  Include directive may appear inside a Match or Host block to perform conditional inclusion.
```
It becomes quite useful to split the configuration into multiple sub-parts for instance as follows: 

```bash
~/.ssh/
├── authorized_keys
├── config        # include directive 'Include config.d/*'
├── config.d/
│   └── 00-general
│   └── 10-private
│   └── 20-home
│   └── 30-work
[...]
```
I was wondering if somehow this splitting ability could not be used (or inherited) within `assh`, typically as follows:
```bash
~/.ssh/
├── authorized_keys
├── config        # generate from 
├── assh.d/
│   └── 00-general.yaml
│   └── 10-private.yaml
│   └── 20-home.yaml
│   └── 30-work.yaml
[...]
```
That might also be a solution to solve #205 ?

issue_title: Random logouts on IIS with FastCGI
issue_body:
I'm running a Flask app on Windows Server 2012 with IIS. This is my first time deploying a Flask app, so I basically followed the steps from [this tutorial][1] and [this one][2]. I installed IIS with CGI, installed wfastcgi in the app's virtual environment, ran wfastcgi-enable as an administrator and specified the FastCGI application as a route handler in web.config using the virtual environment's python executable and wfastcgi.py file, etc. 

That all went smoothly and the app is up and running, but my sessions have been all over the place. I'm using flask-login to login users and flask-ldap3-login to authenticate, following this [first quick start example][3] pretty closely. I'm using flask-login's remember me functionality to persist the login after browser close, which worked perfectly when I was running the app on Flask's built-in server, but only works periodically now. The app redirects to login if current_user is None or current_user.is_authenticated is false. Sometimes I can refresh 20 minutes after logging in and it reloads the home page, whereas other times I'll refresh after a minute and it'll redirect to login. I configured the CGI timeout in the IIS Manager to be 8 hrs in case it was enforcing a shorter timeout, but that hasn't made a difference, the user is still getting logged out randomly.

Has anyone else experienced this? I'm open to any ideas on why I'm experiencing strange session behavior with IIS and FastCGI, or even just how to better debug it. Thank you! 

  [1]: http://blog.mattwoodward.com/2016/07/running-django-application-on-windows.html
  [2]: http://netdot.co/2015/03/09/flask-on-iis/
  [3]: http://flask-ldap3-login.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quick_start.html

issue_title: Support params as dict (with keywords)
issue_body:
Currently, the parser (JSON in this case) only allows for list-style params. As the RFC allows for params to be provided by-position (as an array) or by-names (as an object/dict), the following change in views.py would accomplish this:
`--- test/python3.4/site-packages/modernrpc/views.py	2017-09-29 17:29:49.783385708 -0400
+++ lib/python3.4/site-packages/modernrpc/views.py	2017-09-29 17:25:58.539645454 -0400
@@ -113,5 +113,8 @@
 
                     # Call the python function associated with the RPC method name
-                    result = rpc_method.execute(*params, **kwargs)
+                    if isinstance(params, list):
+                        result = rpc_method.execute(*params, **kwargs)
+                    else:
+                        result = rpc_method.execute(params, **kwargs)
`
The parser itself probably does not need to be changed since the default empty params as [] (list) wouldn't affect the method being called.

issue_title: RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded in comparison
issue_body:
Hello,

The way I create the flask app is the following. I have:
```
/manage.py
/appl/__init.py__
```

Where `manage.py` contains:
```
  from appl import manager
  manager.run()
```

And `__init__.py` contains:
```
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from flask_script import Manager
from flask_migrate import Migrate, MigrateCommand
from flask_security import Security, SQLAlchemyUserDatastore

app = Flask(__name__, static_url_path = "")
app.config.from_pyfile('config.py')

db = SQLAlchemy(app)
migrate = Migrate(app, db)
manager = Manager(app)
manager.add_command('db', MigrateCommand)

# for some reason this lines have to be in here
from appl import views, models

# Setup Flask-Security
user_datastore = SQLAlchemyUserDatastore(db, models.User, models.Role)
security = Security(app, user_datastore)
```

With this configuration, whenever I try to visit `/register` I get:
`RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded in comparison`

I am using python 3.6. I used to use this same approach in the past with python2.7 and former flask-security versions and worked without errors. 

Any ideas about how could I solve this issue?

Thanks!

issue_title: DB connection hangs with regular intervals
issue_body:
I am using Nginx + Gunicorn + Django 1.10.7 for the web.
I am using django-pyodbc-azure + pyodbc + unixodbc + freetds to connect to my database which is MS SQL Server 2016.

The reason I am opening an issue is because the duration of the "hanging" moments are regular. They either last:

- 3 seconds
- 9 seconds
- 30 seconds
- 40 seconds
- Indefinitely

Those intervals were observed accross many requests and were always the same ±20ms.

My django-pyodbc-azure settings are:

- AUTOCOMMIT explicitly set to True
- DATABASE_CONNECTION_POOLING set to False

My gunicorn settings are:

- 8 concurrent workers
- max-requests 4 or 10 (both yield similar results)

Removing max requests to avoid recycling Gunicorn's workers did not change anything.

Some requests use django.db.connection.close() to avoid forking the db conmection when using Python's multiprocessing pool.

The function called in the pool doesn't use the DB.

The SQL Server instance (on another machine) is stable an d works in SQL Management Studio while the hang occurs even if the hanging never stops.

Two request made at the same time are not guaranteed to get the same amount of hanging. If the hanging is indefinite, the request will never return until a timeout occurs.

The requests made to the DB range from opening a cursor to SELECTing over 10 000 rows. The latter shows a higher probability of indefinite hanging but some were observed after opening a cursor.

Few errors are thrown, but I sporadically receive "Read from server failed" and "Cursor's connection closed"

Any help is appreciated, I will update the issue with config and sample code as soon as I can.

```py
        user = get_object_or_404(queryset, pk=pk)
        serializer = UserSerializer(user)
        return Response(serializer.data)
```

Test
```py
def generate_fake_user(pk, name):
    return {
        'pk': pk,
        'email': '{}@example.com'.format(name),
        'password': 'password{}'.format(name),
    }

class UserViewSetTestCase(TestCase):
    users = MockSet(
        User(**generate_fake_user(1, 'A')),
        User(**generate_fake_user(2, 'B')))
    user_objects = patch(
        'app.models.user.User.objects.all',
        return_value=users)

    @user_objects
    def test_retrieve(self, usersMock):
        request = RequestFactory()
        request.user = AnonymousUser()
        result = UserViewSet().retrieve(request)
```

This raises
```
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'DoesNotExist'
```

I'm trying to test the situation where no user can be found for the pk queried, and `get_object_or_404` uses `except queryset.model.DoesNotExist:`
The problem with that queryset is a **MockSet**, which apparently doesn't handle model for parameter.

issue_title: "get_follows" should get all follows by a user
issue_body:
There should be an option for `users. get_follows()` to get all users that a user follows. The Twitch API limits each call to 100, but you can use the `offset` key to technically get all follows. 

So, this library should simplify this process, by adding an option. If the option is enabled, then the library will get the first 100 follows, then the next 100, etc. until they are all retrieved.

issue_title: FormData should not be passed to $http config as data
issue_body:
Method `append` undefined on the **FormData** instance passed to the `formDataAppender` function.

Steps to reproduce:
  1 - Install angular-http-case-converter
  2 - Install angular-upload-file
  3 - Configure an input file as the [rails example](https://github.com/danialfarid/angular-file-upload/wiki/Rails-Example) (with the formDataAppender function)
  4 - Run the application and try to upload something

angular-upload-file version: 2.1.1
angular: 1.3.3
[angular-http-case-converter](https://bitbucket.org/laboratorium_ee/angular-http-case-converter.git): 0.1.1

**Long explanation**:

I'm using [angular-http-case-converter](https://bitbucket.org/laboratorium_ee/angular-http-case-converter.git) to normalize formats between JavaScript (camelCase) and Ruby (snake-case), so during the transformation process it creates a new object transfering the properties of the original object to the new one while converting their format.

The problem is that the object being passed when I use angular-file-upload is **FormData** and this process replaces the original object making an error occur when the request transformer calls the `append` method that doesn't exist on the new object created by the interceptor that converted the format.

I am having trouble with automatically indexing newly created objects with `groupby`.

Every time a new model is created, it should index it.

However, it only indexes the "title" value. It does not index the "tag".

So, I created a manual function `manual_index`. When I run that, the "tag" value does get added to the index. However this process has to be manually triggered.

How can I get the "tag" value to save every time a new object is created?

***Search.py***

```
class TaskIndex(DocType):
    title = String()
    class Meta:
        index = 'task-index'

def manual_index():
    TaskIndex.init()
    es = Elasticsearch()
    bulk(client=es, actions=(b.indexing() for b in models.Task.objects.all().iterator()))
```


**Models.py**


```
from itertools import groupby

class Tag(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField("Name", max_length=5000, blank=True)
    taglevel = models.IntegerField("Tag level", null=True, blank=True)

class Item(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField("Title", max_length=10000, blank=True)
    tag = models.ManyToManyField('Tag', blank=True)

    def get_grouped_tags(self):
        tag = self.tag.order_by('taglevel')
        grouped_tags = {
            tag_level: [
                { 'name': tag_of_level.name, 'taglevel': tag_of_level.taglevel, }
                for tag_of_level in tags_of_level
            ] for tag_level, tags_of_level
            in groupby(tag, lambda tag: tag.taglevel)
        }
        return grouped_tags

    def indexing(self):
        obj = TaskIndex(
            meta={'id': self.id},
            title=self.title,
            tag=self.get_grouped_tags()
        obj.save()
        return obj.to_dict(include_meta=True)
```

issue_title: [Feature Request] Formatting By Significant Figures
issue_body:
The default formatting method is `precision`, which is the digits after the decimal point, but it leads to different levels of details of different numbers. Let's say, if we set `precision=0`, the number `4.23195e-4` and `4.23195e-3` will become:
```
423u
```
and
```
4m
```
correspondingly.

What I prefer is to print the numbers by significant digits, like:
```
>>> eng = lambda x: EngNumber(x, significant=3)
>>> eng(4.23195e-4)
'423u'
>>> eng(4.23195e-3)
'4.23m'
```

issue_title: Demo program incompatible with Python 2.6, Python 3
Unbounded memory consumption with redis asgi
issue_body:
We've noticed our server running out of memory due to concurrent incoming requests outstripping Daphne's ability to keep up with response writing. This is probably related to issue #65.

This can be tested relatively easily by initializing a new Django project and then replace `urls.py` with this example:

```python
from django.conf.urls import url
from django.http import HttpResponse

def testview(request):
    # Largish response required
    return HttpResponse('x' * 10000)

urlpatterns = [
    url(r'^test/', testview),
]

# No special routing
channel_routing = []
```

Add to `settings.py`:

```python
CHANNEL_LAYERS = {
    'redis': {
        'BACKEND': 'asgi_redis.RedisChannelLayer',
        'ROUTING': 'memorytest.urls.channel_routing',
    }
}
```

Then spin up redis, daphne and one worker, and hit it with for example [siege](https://www.joedog.org/siege-home/):

```sh
$ siege -c100 http://localhost:8000/test/
```

Now, watch `redis-server` eat up more and more memory. In some cases (but not all?), `daphne` also starts eating up more and more memory.

This is with 100 concurrent clients. Either adding more clients or increasing the response size make the memory use grow faster.

I don't really understand what is going wrong here. It looks like the workers are producing data faster than Daphne is able to respond.

Of course, this is a very synthetic test. In reality I use `django.http.FileResponse` to stream a file of 30MB or so to tens of clients and this is causing both Daphne and Redis to use 1GB of memory before the OOM killer gets invoked.

```
import mxnet as mx
from mxnet import gluon, autograd, nd

class Binarize(mx.operator.CustomOp):
    def forward(self, is_train, req, in_data, out_data, aux):
        """Implements forward computation.

        is_train : bool, whether forwarding for training or testing.
        req : list of {'null', 'write', 'inplace', 'add'}, how to assign to out_data. 'null' means skip assignment, etc.
        in_data : list of NDArray, input data.
        out_data : list of NDArray, pre-allocated output buffers.
        aux : list of NDArray, mutable auxiliary states. Usually not used.
        """
        x = in_data[0]
        #y = nd.sign(x)
        y = x
        self.assign(out_data[0], req[0], y)

    def backward(self, req, out_grad, in_data, out_data, in_grad, aux):
        """Implements backward computation

        req : list of {'null', 'write', 'inplace', 'add'}, how to assign to in_grad
        out_grad : list of NDArray, gradient w.r.t. output data.
        in_grad : list of NDArray, gradient w.r.t. input data. This is the output buffer.
        """
        x = in_data[0]
        dy = out_grad[0]
        dx = dy*(nd.abs(x) <= 1)
        self.assign(in_grad[0], req[0], dx)

@mx.operator.register("binarize")  # register with name "sigmoid"
class BinarizeProp(mx.operator.CustomOpProp):
    def __init__(self):
        super(BinarizeProp, self).__init__(True)

    def list_arguments(self):
        #  this can be omitted if you only have 1 input.
        return ['data']

    def list_outputs(self):
        #  this can be omitted if you only have 1 output.
        return ['output']

    def infer_shape(self, in_shapes):
        """Calculate output shapes from input shapes. This can be
        omited if all your inputs and outputs have the same shape.

        in_shapes : list of shape. Shape is described by a tuple of int.
        """
        data_shape = in_shapes[0]
        output_shape = data_shape
        # return 3 lists representing inputs shapes, outputs shapes, and aux data shapes.
        return (data_shape,), (output_shape,), ()

    def create_operator(self, ctx, in_shapes, in_dtypes):
        #  create and return the CustomOp class.
        return Binarize()

class BinarizeBlock(gluon.HybridBlock):
    def hybrid_forward(self, F, x):
        return F.Custom(x, name='binarize', op_type='binarize')

class BinaryConv(Conv2D):
    def hybrid_forward(self, F, x, weight, bias=None):
        bin_w = F.Custom(weight, name='binarize', op_type='binarize')
        return super(BinaryConv, self).hybrid_forward(F, x, weight=bin_w, bias=bias)

net = gluon.nn.Sequential()
net.add(BinaryConv(channels=20, kernel_size=5, activation='relu'))
net.collect_params().initialize(mx.init.Xavier(magnitude=2.24), ctx=mx.cpu())

test = nd.random.normal(shape=(1, 3, 224, 224))
net(test)
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Simply run above code

## What have you tried to solve it?

1. Tried to find issues with shape inference in the custom function, but everything seems fine. Using BinarizeBlock in sequential does not cause an issue, it is only when the custom function is used in BinaryConv that the above error appears. I don't see any obvious reasons this issue is popping up, since it looks like the infer_shape function of Binarize is working. I'd love to get this figured out!

issue_title: Embedding Backward (AddTakeGradLargeBatchCaller) non-deterministic nan values
issue_body:
## Description

The `AddTakeGradLargeBatchCaller` operator called during backward of Embedding is broken and results in `nan` at random positions in the gradient array. 

## Environment info (Required)
- Cuda 9.0 or Cuda 9.2 with respective mxnet-cu92, mxnet-cu90 prebuilt binaries (both v1.2 and nightly affected)
- EC2 p3.2xlarge or p2.xlarge instance


While it is occurs rarely with Cuda 9.0 or p2.xlarge it almost always occurs on Cuda 9.2 and p3.2xlarge. 

## Minimum reproducible example

``` python
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np

N = 50000
ctx = mx.gpu()

embedding = mx.gluon.nn.Embedding(N, 300)
embedding.initialize(ctx=ctx)
i = 0
np.random.seed(1)
idx = mx.nd.array(np.random.randint(0, N, size=(1024, 160)), ctx=ctx)

got_nan = False
while True:
    i += 1
    with mx.autograd.record():
        emb_in = embedding(idx)
        loss = emb_in.sum()
    loss.backward()

    if not np.all(np.isfinite(embedding.weight.grad().asnumpy())):
        nan_rows = np.where(~np.isfinite(embedding.weight.grad().asnumpy()))[0]
        print(f'Got nan {i}\tRetrying with same data. '
              f'(Affected rows: {nan_rows.tolist()}).')
        got_nan = True
    else:
        if got_nan:  # We got nan before and it disappeared now
            print(f'nan disappeared in {i}..')
            break

    if i % 100 == 0:
        print(f'{i}')

```

## Steps to reproduce

Run above script with cuda 9.2 and observe very frequent nan values:

``` 
% python debug_embedding_nan.py
Got nan 3       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [14721, 14721], [1, 2]).
Got nan 4       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [20, 20, 39, 39, 18232, 18232], [257, 258, 1, 2, 1, 2]).
Got nan 5       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [20, 20, 71, 33346, 38015], [257, 258, 258, 130, 130]).
Got nan 6       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [20, 20], [257, 258]).
nan disappeared in 7..
% python debug_embedding_nan.py 
Got nan 7       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [20, 20, 33, 71, 71, 71, 71, 71], [257, 258, 1, 1, 2, 129, 130, 258]).
nan disappeared in 8..
% python debug_embedding_nan.py
Got nan 1       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [1489], [129]).
Got nan 2       Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [42581, 42581], [257, 258]).
nan disappeared in 3..

```

Run above script with cuda 9.0 and observe (infrequent) nan values:

``` 
100
200
300
400
500
600
700
800
900
1000
1100
1200
1300
1400
Got nan 1461    Retrying with same data. (Affected indices: [3254], [2]).
nan disappeared in 1462..

```


## What have you tried to solve it?

1. Apply the following patch and set
``` patch
From 3fd91f0078e70cf990ce1549081c03cfb50292ad Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Leonard Lausen <leonard@lausen.nl>
Date: Fri, 15 Jun 2018 18:45:39 +0000
Subject: [PATCH] MXNET_FORCE_ADDTAKEGRAD to disable
 AddTakeGradLargeBatchCaller

If MXNET_FORCE_ADDTAKEGRAD is set, EmbeddingOpBackward will always use
AddTakeGrad independently of gradient input and output shape
---
 src/operator/tensor/indexing_op.h | 6 +++++-
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/operator/tensor/indexing_op.h b/src/operator/tensor/indexing_op.h
index 87381960e..d3a1bdfd6 100644
--- a/src/operator/tensor/indexing_op.h
+++ b/src/operator/tensor/indexing_op.h
@@ -598,7 +598,11 @@ void EmbeddingOpBackward(const nnvm::NodeAttrs& attrs,
         uint64_t shape_out_prod =
           static_cast<uint64_t>(grad_out.shape_[0])*
           static_cast<uint64_t>(grad_out.shape_[1]);
-        if (shape_out_prod < (uint64_t)16384 && shape_in_prod < (uint64_t)16384) {
+
+        const char *type = getenv("MXNET_FORCE_ADDTAKEGRAD");
+        const bool default_addtakegrad = (type == nullptr);
+
+        if (!default_addtakegrad || ( shape_out_prod < (uint64_t)16384 && shape_in_prod < (uint64_t)16384 )) {
           AddTakeGrad(grad_in, data, grad_out);
         } else {
           AddTakeGradLargeBatchCaller(ctx, grad_in, data, grad_out);
--
2.17.1

```

2. Run above script when `MXNET_FORCE_ADDTAKEGRAD=1` is exported.

The installation instructions for Mac, refer you to this script:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmlc/mxnet/master/setup-utils/install-mxnet-osx-python.sh

If "fails" at the end during a test.

## Environment info (Required)

macOS 10.12.6 (Sierra)

MXNet commit hash:
I chose to use the v1.1.0 tag for the install, so this is:
07a83a0325a3d782513a04f47d711710972cb144

## Error Message:
```

:-(
```

```
8c8590217d260a:mxnet markhama$ python
Python 2.7.10 (default, Feb  7 2017, 00:08:15)

Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

>>> quit()
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Set env var: `export MXNET_TAG=1.1.0`
2. Run script: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dmlc/mxnet/master/setup-utils/install-mxnet-osx-python.sh`

## Comments

I reran this script using environments and a Python3 setup. It passed the test, and I don't get weird errors when doing `import mxnet`. Note that doing a vanilla Mac install, it tries to bind to Mac's default 2.7.10 Python... and apparently fails.

issue_title: configparser requirement not in setup.py
issue_body:
While testing what I uploaded to Test PyPI, I did `detect-secrets --help` (on Python 2), and got:

`ImportError: No module named configparser`

`flake8`, [`requirements.txt`](https://github.com/Yelp/detect-secrets/blob/master/requirements-dev.txt) requires `configparser`, so we never ran into this during `make test`, 😮  pretty sneaky!

We should make a `make test w/ setup.py dependencies only` option from the Makefile.

But for now, [`python-future`](http://python-future.org/compatible_idioms.html#configparser) suggest doing `from configparser import ConfigParser` so that is what I will do.
