issue_title: Critical serialization error - Inherited objects do not contain all attributes in list
issue_body:
```python
class User(BaseModel):
    email = EmailField(api_name='Email', required=True)
...

class NaturalUser(User):
    first_name = CharField(api_name='FirstName', required=True)
...
```

```python
allUsers = Users.all() 
# returns [
#  {
#    "PersonType": "NATURAL", 
#    "FirstName": "MyName",
#    ...
#  }]

assertIsInstance(users[0], NaturalUser) # OK
self.assertIsNotNone(users[0].first_name) # FAIL
```
Extended objects should be serialized correctly and contain all the attributes, as received from the server.

In order to reveal the issue, I added a test 6aabacdfcf7d5b3063c498706d033b9656baa67d that [fails on travis](https://travis-ci.org/Mangopay/mangopay2-python-sdk/jobs/230610405)

causes this exception:

```
[...]/profitbricks/client.py in create_lan(self, datacenter_id, lan)
    597         data = self._underscore_to_camelcase(
```

ipython says the function signature (in both lob-python v1.0.0 and lob v1.1) is

```python
lob.Postcard.create(cls, name, to, message=None, back=None, from_address=None, **kwargs)
```
meaning the example code like this doesn't work at all:

```python
response = lob.Postcard.create(
    to_address={
        'name': 'John Doe',
        'address_line1': '404 Building Apt. 404',
        'address_line2': '',
        'address_city': 'New York',

    }
)
```

```python
TypeError: create() takes at least 3 arguments (3 given)
```

```python
response = lob.Postcard.create(
    name='My Test Postcard Mailer',

        'specialty': 'abcdefg',
    }
)
```
```python
InvalidRequestError: [{u'status_code': 422, u'message': u'metadata must be an object'}]
```
So then I tried
```python
class MetaData:
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        for k, v in kwargs.iteritems():
            setattr(self, k, v)

lob.Postcard.create(..., metadata=Metadata(**{'id': r.id, 'specialty': 'abcdefg'}), ...)
```
still doesn't work:
```python
AttributeError: MetaData instance has no attribute '__len__'
```

Any suggestions?

issue_title: disassociate_first optional flag
issue_body:
Feature request: There are several use cases where it is desirable that a newly launched instance can be guaranteed to have/steal an address from a pool of existing addresses that may all be attached.  Our use cases is rolling docker deployments. #15 is similar. 

A flag like "disassociate_first" that will simply perform a disassociate call on the elastic IP
http://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/disassociate-address.html
 before trying to acquire the address would seemingly solve our problem.  If the flag is false by default it shouldn't be an issue to any existing users.

issue_title: Proposal/epic: Rename "docker-py" to "Docker SDK for Python"
issue_body:
### Proposal

As part of a wider initiative, we're creating some properly supported SDKs for the Engine API.

docker-py was the first official-ish library, all that time ago. [Go is now the second](https://godoc.org/github.com/docker/docker/client). However, if we're going to continue to create ones for other languages, it'd be nice if they had consistent naming, roughly consistent APIs, and so on. It is also helpful to be able to refer them as a collective product, rather than the wishy-washy "client libraries for the Engine API".

Hence, the "Docker SDKs". Each particular language will be "Docker SDK for X": Docker SDK for Python, Docker SDK for Go, etc. [There will be centralised documentation](https://github.com/docker/docker.github.io/pull/210) with [a getting started guide for multiple languages (and curl!)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bfirsh/docker.github.io/21c4a7407f4782bc6337213fb8fa2bc2253163d2/api/getting-started.md), then linking off to each library's respective reference documentation.

### Tasks

- [ ] Make it really clear that "docker-py" is now "Docker SDK for Python". All old URLs should redirect, all landing pages should mention that it has renamed, and so on.
- [ ] Replace all mentions of "docker-py" in the source code with "Docker SDK for Python" or "docker-sdk-python".
- [ ] Move the GitHub repository to docker/docker-sdk-python. (Update Travis, Jenkins, etc, too.)
- [ ] Move the documentation to https://docker-sdk-python.readthedocs.io. Ensure the old documentation redirects correctly.
- [ ] Release 2.0 as the PyPi package "docker", not "docker-py". "docker-py" will remain for 1.x.

issue_title: Access response headers in "get_iterator_next_request_kwargs"
issue_body:
Some API's (Githubs for instance) pass info required to define the "next" endpoint to be fetched in response headers.
Given this, response headers should be available in ```get_iterator_next_request_kwargs```.
For this to work, it's needed to pass the raw response to the ```TapiocaClient``` generated after a request and change the method definition of ```get_iterator_next_request_kwargs```.

Here are some of the possible changes that can be made:
- Pass ```response``` as a kwarg
````python
def get_iterator_next_request_kwargs(self, iterator_request_kwargs, response_data, response=None):
    ...
```
- remove ```response_data``` and pass only ```response```
```python
def get_iterator_next_request_kwargs(self, iterator_request_kwargs, response):
    ...
```

This discussion was initiated by @gileno

> This refers to a function using its python name.

issue_title: Mismatch between hosted NER model and "Creating a Model" Tutorial?
issue_body:
Hi!

On http://allennlp.org/models , in the NER section, it notes that "This model is the "baseline" model described in Peters, Ammar, Bhagavatula, and Power 2017." and that "This is also the model constructed in our Creating a Model tutorial."

After downloading the hosted model and peeking at its config, it looks like it uses some constraints on the CRF that Joel recently added. This doesn't appear in the config at [tutorials/getting_started/crf_tagger.json](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/tutorials/getting_started/crf_tagger.json), so these two models are not the same (contrary to what the site notes). 

I'm not sure if the baseline model in Peters 2017 uses these constraints either. Maybe you want to consider rewording the description of the model on the site and/or adding the constraints to the tutorial config file? Not sure which you would prefer to do.

issue_title: MongoDB collector splits logically atomic metric key components
issue_body:
Hi!
There was an issue #547, where @bdelbosc notices, that mongo db metrics contains spaces and fixes that. But at the same time he replaced dashes with dots.
That's strange because a lot of metric names contain dashes and logically they must be written as single element in metric key.
We also have dashes in collection and index names, so writing graphite functions on top of these metrics is hard.

So I think, replacing dashes with dots completly wrong. That's easily fixable, but fixing this is breaking change. What do you think?

issue_title: Faster MSD calculations
issue_body:
I found a Python implementation to compute MSD with FFT which seems faster than conventional MSD algorithm because the algorithm is `O(N*log(N))`.

I did a quick and small benchmark here : https://github.com/hadim/public_notebooks/blob/master/quick_msd_calculations/quick_msd_calculations.ipynb

Two things : 

- the returned `DataFrame` is less detailled than in `tp.msd`.

- the msd array is sliced at the end of the function to take into account `max_lagtime` parameter which is really not efficient ! I didn't find a way to integrate it in the algorithm and I don't know wether it's possible.

Would you be interested by a PR to integrate it into trackpy ?

If you do, we also need to discuss how to inegrate it with `tp.imsd` (maybe with a `fft=False` default parameter ?).

Edit : I added `max_lagtime` into the loop. According to the author of the algorithm it's not possible do things faster with `max_lagtime`.

issue_title: Examples might be part of unit testing process
issue_body:
Hello,

to catch issue like https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/issues/96

I think examples should be in a given directory (`examples` for example)
and be used as unit tests

For example:

``` python
import datapackage

dp = datapackage.DataPackage('http://data.okfn.org/data/core/gdp/datapackage.json')
brazil_gdp = [{'Year': int(row['Year']), 'Value': float(row['Value'])}
              for row in dp.resources[0].data if row['Country Code'] == 'BRA']

max_gdp = max(brazil_gdp, key=lambda x: x['Value'])
min_gdp = min(brazil_gdp, key=lambda x: x['Value'])
percentual_increase = max_gdp['Value'] / min_gdp['Value']

msg = (
    'The highest Brazilian GDP occured in {max_gdp_year}, when it peaked at US$ '
    '{max_gdp:1,.0f}. This was {percentual_increase:1,.2f}% more than its '
    'minimum GDP in {min_gdp_year}.'
).format(max_gdp_year=max_gdp['Year'],
         max_gdp=max_gdp['Value'],
         percentual_increase=percentual_increase,
         min_gdp_year=min_gdp['Year'])

print(msg)
# The highest Brazilian GDP occured in 2011, when it peaked at US$ 2,615,189,973,181. This was 172.44% more than its minimum GDP in 1960.
```

might be stored in a file `examples/reading.py` and have a `main` function so script can be run but also be import without being run

``` python
import datapackage


def main():
    dp = datapackage.DataPackage('http://data.okfn.org/data/core/gdp/datapackage.json')
    brazil_gdp = [{'Year': int(row['Year']), 'Value': float(row['Value'])}
                  for row in dp.resources[0].data if row['Country Code'] == 'BRA']

    max_gdp = max(brazil_gdp, key=lambda x: x['Value'])
    min_gdp = min(brazil_gdp, key=lambda x: x['Value'])
    percentual_increase = max_gdp['Value'] / min_gdp['Value']

    msg = (
        'The highest Brazilian GDP occured in {max_gdp_year}, when it peaked at US$ '
        '{max_gdp:1,.0f}. This was {percentual_increase:1,.2f}% more than its '
        'minimum GDP in {min_gdp_year}.'
    ).format(max_gdp_year=max_gdp['Year'],
             max_gdp=max_gdp['Value'],
             percentual_increase=percentual_increase,
             min_gdp_year=min_gdp['Year'])

    print(msg)
    # The highest Brazilian GDP occured in 2011, when it peaked at US$ 2,615,189,973,181. This was 172.44% more than its minimum GDP in 1960.

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

in `tests/test_examples.py`

we should have

``` python
from examples.reading import reading


def test_reading():
    result = reading.main()
```

Testing could be refined by adding some asserts...

Kind regards

issue_title: Consider switching to html5parser
issue_body:
`lxml` has an html5parser that can handle some of the inanities that bad HTML pages present. 

For example, this page:

http://media.ca11.uscourts.gov/opinions/unpub/logname.php?begin=9720&num=485&numBegin=1

Has random less than signs in some of the docket numbers, which results in a terrible HTML tree. I was able to solve that in ca11_u, which fixes the problem and even preserves the API for the scrapers.

The hard part of this is that the html5parser's `fromstring` function returns `_Element` objects while the `html.fromstring` function returns `HtmlElement`s. I was able to get around this in ca11_u with something like:

    from lxml.html import fromstring, tostring
    from lxml.html import html5parser

    e = html5parser.fromstring(text)
    html_element = fromstring(tostring(e))

Though that involves an extra parse and an extra serialization, all of which sucks and obscures.

issue_title: Apply Release Engineering and Pin Versions of Required Libraries
issue_body:
You should explicitly list all required libraries, including `requests`, here:

https://github.com/AuthorizeNet/sdk-python/blob/8eec6ea2941d5c35d08082d77c2b6800fd5192f9/setup.py#L66

And you are being negligent by not specifying versions of each library you use.  If you do not pin versions of libraries, it is unclear what your test cases actually mean when they passed---we do not know what versions of libraries your software works with.

Since commits are intermittent on this project, I highly recommend you pin the versions so that we don't have to worry about grabbing a new version that breaks your library---and in the worst case silently.

```

`fleetctl cat example_v56.run.12.service`:

```
[Unit]

[Service]
ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "IMAGE=$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/host 2>&1):$(etcdctl get /deis/registry/port 2>&1)/example:v56; docker pull $IMAGE"

issue_title: Replace global excepthook with high level try-except
issue_body:
For some reason, an early version of the CLI used `sys.excepthook` to do global error handling.
The goal was to hide any exceptions which occur from the end user, and present a clean "minimal" error message that wouldn't be too scary.

This is all well and good, but it should just be a try-except block in the main command method.
Any exceptions thrown and handled by parsing are presented nicely already anyway.

issue_title: `globus transfer async-transfer` and `globus-transfer async-delete` should print out the Task ID in text mode
issue_body:
The textual output from the task submission commands doesn't include the Task ID -- just the "message" field back from the API.

This is a usability problem, as immediately after submission, starting something like a `globus transfer task wait` requires doing a task listing.
The JSON output includes all of the fields, so this isn't a problem for scripting, but rather for interactive use.

The text output format should probably be something like this:
```shell
$ globus transfer async-transfer ...
Message: The transfer has been accepted and a task has been created and queued for execution
Task ID:    aaaaa-bbbbb-ccccc-ddddd
```

That's just for consistency with the various other parts of the CLI that output multi-field text info in a non-columnar format.
The alternative is to ditch that format and go with
```
$ globus transfer async-transfer ...
The transfer has been accepted and a task has been created and queued for execution
Task ID:    aaaaa-bbbbb-ccccc-ddddd
```

To me, those two are indistinguishable for a user, so we should just go with the one that matches other existing output.

issue_title: Fix CI to skip slow tests.
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The slowest tests are marked here:

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/tests/json-fixtures/test_state.py#L62-L113

* These tests **should not** be executed in CI runs initiated by pull requests.
* These tests **should** execute in the daily cron test run.

The test suite uses the `TRAVIS_EVENT_TYPE` to determine if it is running in the `cron` context.

### How can it be fixed

Investigate why this isn't behaving as expected since it looks like the Byzantium test suite is running long when it should be around 5-10 minutes runtime.

issue_title: Deduplication of `Message.gas_price` and `Message.origin`
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The `Message.gas_price` and `Message.origin` are copied for every level of computation during VM execution, despite them remaining *constant* for the entire context of the VM execution.

It would be nice to remove this duplication in favor of having these values stored in *one* location.

Also see: `sighash` from https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/259/files


### How can it be fixed

Implement a `TransactionContext` object, similar to the `ExecutionContext` object introduced in #273 

This would be an object that we treat as immutable which houses information like `gas_price`, and `sighash` for the in-progress sharding work.

Since the info for this object will change over time with changes to the VM rules we should use the following API to allow for changing out the class which contains this data.

```
# vm_state.py

class VMState:
    transaction_context_class = None  # must be set in individual VMState classes

    @classmethod
    def get_transaction_context_class(cls):
        # error check to be sure it's set
        return self.transaction_context_class
```

Then, within the `VMState.execute_transaction` implementation, after the `message` instance is created, we should also generate the transaction context instance.

Then, we modify `vm_state.get_computation` to take both `message` and `transaction_context` as arguments.   The `transaction_context` should be stored on the `Computation` object.  When child computations are spawned, this object should be passed into those instances.

Any opcodes which previously accessed values from the `Message` which now reside on the `TransactionContext` object will need to be updated.

good_args = (
    '0x3db0a82491cca2b89e61db4c1885a8e745c63265',
    1,
    1,
    1,
    28,
    to_bytes(28959435470761257621766426729547047597677396750272393780686032384044109160873),
    to_bytes(35812761560685972683592328664071490125564303384467730161104237647446031473187),
)
bad_args = (
    '0x3db0a82491cca2b89e61db4c1885a8e745c63265',
    1,
    1,
    1,
    28,
    28959435470761257621766426729547047597677396750272393780686032384044109160873,
    35812761560685972683592328664071490125564303384467730161104237647446031473187,
)

assert find_matching_fn_abi(abi, name, args=good_args, kwargs={})  # pass
assert find_matching_fn_abi(abi, name, args=bad_args, kwargs={})  # raises `ValueError: No matching functions found`
```

Actually it's not problem of web3 I guess but maybe it's possible to improve a validation and encoding there, because after all it raises validation error on contract call:

```
  ....
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/web3/utils/normalizers.py", line 121, in abi_address_to_hex
    validate_address(data)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/web3/utils/validation.py", line 116, in validate_address
    raise InvalidAddress("Address has an invalid EIP checksum", value)
web3.exceptions.InvalidAddress: ('Address has an invalid EIP checksum', '0x3db0a82491cca2b89e61db4c1885a8e745c63265')
```

Some kind of validation applies two times and working in different way when try to find method in ABI and when creates a transaction. Isn't?

Maybe could you advise me how to encode automatically a python primitives for any contract method without inspect ABI? I'm sure web3 does it for itself.

issue_title: deactivate env fails on OS X for PyStan (uses new compilers)
issue_body:
I'm attempting to create an environment in which to use PyStan. PyStan creates Python C/C++ modules on-the-fly and so uses the Anaconda compilers. I'm trying to keep my base env clean in terms of paths to standard compilers (like clang); the PyStan env changes `clang` to point to Anaconda's compiler rather than Apple's.

I have Miniconda (Py-3) newly installed, and I created the env this way:

`conda create -n py36stan python=3.6 anaconda pystan`

Activating it appears to work fine:

```
$ source activate py36stan
INFO: activate_clang_osx-64.sh made the following environmental changes:
+AR=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-ar
+AS=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-as
+CC=x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang
+CFLAGS=-march=core2 -mtune=haswell -mssse3 -ftree-vectorize -fPIC -fPIE -fstack-protector-strong -O2 -pipe
+CHECKSYMS=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-checksyms
+CLANG=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang
+CODESIGN_ALLOCATE=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-codesign_allocate
+CONDA_BUILD_SYSROOT=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX10.13.sdk
...
```

Deactivating fails:

```
$ source deactivate
ERROR: This cross-compiler package contains no program /bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-ar
ERROR: deactivate_clang_osx-64.sh failed, see above for details
ERROR: This cross-compiler package contains no program /bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang++
ERROR: deactivate_clangxx_osx-64.sh failed, see above for details

$ echo $AR
/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-a
```

Note the last; $AR did not exist in the base env; it is created for the new one and removing it is evidently aborted when `deactivate` fails.

I find the error messages unclear. I don't know what package they refer to. I also don't know if they refer to a problem in the new py36stan env or in the base env.

I realize Anaconda 5 has new, arch-specific compilers, implementing a kind of cross-compilation capability:  [Utilizing the new compilers...](https://www.anaconda.com/blog/developer-blog/utilizing-the-new-compilers-in-anaconda-distribution-5/?lang=en-us). I don't fully understand when they need to be used and when/whether the Apple compilers mentioned on that page need to be installed. I do know that `clang_osx-64` and `clang_osx-64` are installed as PyStan dependencies, and that they put a bunch of `x86_64-apple-darwin...` tools in the /bin directory of the env. I tried installing these two clang packages in my base env before creating the PyStan env, but the deactivate failure still happens.

Is there something I'm neglecting to do with `conda`, or is this is a bug?

issue_title: new dependencies for 4.2
issue_body:
* click, first to refine progressbar, and later as a more sophisticated argparse
* toolz, because conda core logic will start to look more and more functional
* boltons, because there's too much quality code not to reuse
* auxlib, finally as a full dependency
* responses, as a test dependency for mocking requests
* enum34, for python27
* futures, for python27
* effect, not for sure yet, but under consideration

Some of these may be vendored like progressbar.

I don't see many dependencies beyond these for 4.X releases.

issue_title: Check permissions first
issue_body:
If you search for "permission" in the issue tracker you'll find lots of people who have suffered greatly from Conda starting to do something and then borking out because of a permission problem:

https://github.com/conda/conda/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+permission

There are too many to list here, and I don't think it would help for me to include the most recent time (just now) when this has happened to me -- because it is one of the variants many other people have already reported.

I would suggest that Conda should check permissions **first**, then warn or abort if there are issues.  A frequent outcome of this problem is an inoperative Anaconda instance, requiring full reinstallation  (there are a few manual recovery strategies, but they require some expert insight into how things work).

Additionally if a permissions problem (or `OSError`) does crop up it should be caught and a helpful error message passed to the user.  Instead right now we return a stack trace.  Yuck.

issue_title: Activate issue with parallel activations - "File exists" in symlinked path
issue_body:
I'm working in a cluster environment where I am trying to activate a conda environment in multiple parallel processes. After recently upgrading to the latest release (4.1.2), I've been receiving a "File exists" error in some of those processes, similar to the below:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/symln_home/anaconda/bin/conda", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/Users/tomflem/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 48, in main
    activate.main()

```

I've been able to repro this issue on a laptop when my PATH to the root anaconda install contains a symlinked directory ("symln_home," in this example). Doesn't seem to be a problem when the PATH is symlink-free. The below has produced the error consistently for me on OS X:

```

```python
from multiprocessing import Pool
import os

call = ['source activate myenv && echo $PATH' for i in range(30)]
pool = Pool(4)
pool.map(os.system, call)
pool.close()
```

Is this a bug, or are there any workarounds for this particular use case? In my cluster environment, my HOME directory (where I've installed anaconda) is symlinked, which I would assume is a fairly common setup in shared computing (I don't admin it, so unfortunately I can't change that aspect).
````

issue_title: Read group change of type when updating read tags
issue_body:
Hi,

the read group type is automatically and wrongly changed from Z to A, if read tags are updated and if the read group is numeric (in my case 1). This leads to problems with GATK (see https://code.google.com/p/pysam/issues/detail?id=92).

Example code:

    from collections import OrderedDict
    
    import pysam
    
    sam_in = pysam.Samfile("/tmp/rgz-to-rga.bam")
    sam_out = pysam.Samfile("/tmp/rgz-to-rga.out.bam", "wb", template=sam_in)
    for r in sam_in:
        tags = OrderedDict(r.tags)
        # pseudo operation on tags
        tags['BD'], tags['BI'] = tags['BI'], tags['BD']
        r.tags = tags.items()
        sam_out.write(r)
    sam_in.close()
    sam_out.close()
    
    samtools view /tmp/rgz-to-rga.out.bam | grep -o 'RG:.:.'
    # RG:A:1
    samtools view /tmp/rgz-to-rga.bam | grep -o 'RG:.:.'
    RG:Z:1

Input file rgz-to-rga.bam (1.4kB) is available here: https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/u/4119940/rgz-to-rga.bam. All it takes is a file with RG ID:1 in the header (which is default for Picard's  AddOrReplaceReadGroups)

I've tried pysam 0.7.7 and 0.8.2.

Andreas

issue_title: Mismatch between expected and actual transport interface
issue_body: The perform_request method of RequestsHTTPConnection and Urllib3HTTPConnection both return three values: status, headers, data. The search method in client/__init__.py expects two values: _, data. The search method fails with "Too many values to unpack".

issue_title: `az storage blob upload-batch`: unclear documentation
issue_body:
### Environment summary

(Same as in my last issue.)

---

### Description

The documentation of `az storage blob upload-batch` is really unclear.

1. It seems to me that the description of the destination argument has a few occurrences of "source" that should really be "target".

2. The doc for the source argument is ungrammatical in a confusing way too.

3. It fails to specify what happens when the source directory has nested directories: is the structure copied? flattened? or maybe subdirs are ignored?

5. It is really unclear what "shape" should a URL destination have.  An example would help a lot.  (I had to read the source, and the only thing I could figure is that it can be the container name, which I used.)

6. It also fails to mention that the destination can be "<container-name>/<subdir>".

7. Slightly unrelated: it'd be nice if the syntax of this would be similar to the syntax of the `upload` subcommand.  Even better, if there was only `upload`, and it had a flag specifying that the source is a glob pattern for several files to upload.

issue_title: [App Service] [Discussion] Provide first-class/simpler support for retrieving FTP info
issue_body:
The new ` az appservice web deployment list-publishing-profiles` command is cool, but I'm not sure whether it's intuitive to look there in order to retrieve FTP info, and if I'm using web apps on Linux, then I'm not sure whether the MSDeploy info would be useful (or even work?). So this command feels a bit "low-level", as opposed to trying to provide a first-class experience for a potentially common scenario (e.g. how do I get FTP access?).

I wonder whether it would be useful to have a command that allowed getting the FTP info specifically, that was effectively the equivalent of running the following command:

```shell
az appservice web deployment list-publishing-profiles --query "[?publishMethod=='FTP'].{URL:publishUrl, Username:userName,Password:userPWD}"
```

Maybe the above is obvious, and it's just me. But I wanted to throw out the discussion topic, and see what folks thought.

issue_title: [App Service] Does the "web list" command need to require a resource group?

issue_title: [App Service] [Discussion] Provide first-class/simpler support for retrieving FTP info
issue_body:
The new ` az appservice web deployment list-publishing-profiles` command is cool, but I'm not sure whether it's intuitive to look there in order to retrieve FTP info, and if I'm using web apps on Linux, then I'm not sure whether the MSDeploy info would be useful (or even work?). So this command feels a bit "low-level", as opposed to trying to provide a first-class experience for a potentially common scenario (e.g. how do I get FTP access?).

I wonder whether it would be useful to have a command that allowed getting the FTP info specifically, that was effectively the equivalent of running the following command:

```shell
az appservice web deployment list-publishing-profiles --query "[?publishMethod=='FTP'].{URL:publishUrl, Username:userName,Password:userPWD}"
```

Something like the following might be simpler and more discoverable (I'm not a fan of this exact naming, so it's just meant to be illustrative):

```shell
az appservice web deployment show-ftp-info
```

Maybe the above is obvious, and it's just me. Or maybe this isn't a common enough scenario to warrant simplifying it. But I wanted to throw out the discussion topic, and see what folks thoughts were here.

issue_title: [Help] Fullstop always added even if terminal point exists
issue_body:
Actual:
```
Commands:
    configure: Interactive experience for setting up the Azure CLI.
    feedback : Loving or hating the CLI?  Let us know!.
```

Expected:
```
Commands:
    configure: Interactive experience for setting up the Azure CLI.
    feedback : Loving or hating the CLI?  Let us know!
```

Instead of just checking for `.`, we should check for `!` and `?` also.

issue_title: [Storage] Blob name should default on download
issue_body: Currently, when downloading a file, the destination filename defaults to the filename on Azure if not specified. Blob download doesn't do this, but should.

issue_title: DNS zone import should strip trailing .@ from relative record names
issue_body:
The following is a legitimate DNS record from a BIND zone file:

_sip._tls.@	3600 	IN	SRV	100	1	443	sipdir.online.lync.com

Note the '.@' in the record name, which currently causes import to fail (Bad Request).

Where a record name ends in .@, we should strip it off (and then apply the current value of $ORIGIN).

issue_title: --ids should support JSON as well as space separated values
issue_body:
Using the CLI on a mac, installed through `brew`

When I run:

```
az resource delete --ids $(az resource list -g aci-builders --query "[].id" --resource-type "Microsoft.ContainerInstance/containerGroups")
```

I get:

```
az resource: error: argument --ids: invalid ResourceId value: '['
```

Which makes sense, given the output of `az resource list -g aci-builders --query "[].id" --resource-type "Microsoft.ContainerInstance/containerGroups"`, however as a user, this doesn't make sense at all - why can't the `--ids` parameter also accept a JSON array of IDs instead of a space-separated string?

issue_title: Wrong Signature String generation because of missing content-type header
issue_body:
I use this package with Blobxfer and I get an authorization error on every call. I use PUT bloc and PUT block list endoints.

The reason is that the Signature String generated by Azure package uses an empty content-type header since there is no default value. Here is how headers values are declared in _put_block
`
request.headers = {
    'x-ms-lease-id': _to_str(lease_id)
}
`

but it seems that the Azure api uses a default value (= "application/x-www-urlencoded") for this header when empty. It falls to an signed signature miss match. The solution would be to set a default Content-Type header for every calls where a default value is used API side when computing the signature

`
request.headers = {
    'x-ms-lease-id': _to_str(lease_id),
    'content-type': 'application/octet-stream'
}
`

issue_title: get_page_ranges_diff timesout on large page blobs
issue_body:
We are seeing read timeout on when `get_page_ranges_diff` is called on page blobs that are either 512G or 1023G (premium storage).

```
  File ".env/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/azure/storage/blob/pageblobservice.py", line 565, in get_page_ranges_diff

    raise ex
azure.common.AzureException: ReadTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='<name>.blob.core.cloudapi.de', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=11)
```
The socket timeout seems to be set to low value of 11s which is where it seems to fail.  Setting timeout to high value on `get_page_ranges_diff` doesn't seem to have any effect.

I've tried setting this to 60s which seemed to have worked but I think `get_page_ranges_diff` should respect the timeout param (and timesout to infinite is None)

issue_title: Assert in multiply2_p8r
issue_body:
See description here:
http://bulletphysics.org/Bullet/phpBB3/viewtopic.php?f=9&t=10413


I managed to avoid the assert by enabling the motors before the first step is made.

Apparently a btGeneric6DofConstraint has its m_numConstraintRows by default set to 0 but m_numConstraintRows is increased by 1 for every linear limit that needs to apply a force or angular limit that needs to apply a torque. For the last check to be positive, the motor needs to be enabled.
For me the assert was called when adding a second hinge constraint in the scene. So even though in other cases m_numConstraintRows of the btGeneric6DofConstraint was zero, it did not call the assert in those cases. So I'm not truly understanding what went wrong and why, but at least things are working :-)

issue_title: memory leak in getJointStates
issue_body:
Apparently there is a memory leak in the **p.getJointStates** method which doesn't occur for the **p.getJointState** method. Especially when using pybullet for reinforcement learning this can cause problems. 

Here is a little test script to reproduce the error: 
[bullet_mem_leak.tar.gz](https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/files/1967018/bullet_mem_leak.tar.gz)

In line 39 the getJointStates method is called, which increases the memory usage gradually. Every 1000 iterations the memory information is printed. If in contrast getJointState is used, there is no memory leak. Additionally by uncommenting line 49 one can see the cause of the leak, a list is created every call of the method which isn't freed afterwards anymore.

issue_title: Pybullet Backend API for Physics & Render Engine Independence
issue_body:
I like the philosophy and approach of [François Chollet with Keras in TensorFlow](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UeheTiBJ0Io) described in the first minute of the linked video, which focuses on reducing complexity and improving ease of use.

While replying to #1138 I realized a backend design might help meet the independence goals of pybullet described in the [pybullet quick start introduction](https://docs.google.com/document/d/10sXEhzFRSnvFcl3XxNGhnD4N2SedqwdAvK3dsihxVUA/edit#heading=h.2ye70wns7io3). I think a complete design would also need at least some of the geometry, linear algebra, realtime fwd+inv kinematics & dynamics described in #1138.

**Pybullet Backend API for independence from the underlying physics and render engine**

Keras provides a good reference design for [multiple backend compatibility](https://keras.io/backend/#keras-backends) (also see the [backend source code link](https://github.com/fchollet/keras/tree/master/keras/backend)) which supports TensorFlow, Theano, and will soon support CNTK. Here is an example from the [Keras backend docs](https://keras.io/backend/):

```python
from keras import backend as K

# This is creating tensors, but it could just as well be immediate execution
b = K.random_uniform_variable(shape=(3, 4)). # Uniform distribution
c = K.random_normal_variable(shape=(3, 4)). # Gaussian distribution
d = K.random_normal_variable(shape=(3, 4)).

# all-zeros variable:
var = K.zeros(shape=(3, 4, 5))
# all-ones:
var = K.ones(shape=(3, 4, 5))

a = b + c * K.abs(d)
c = K.dot(a, K.transpose(b))
a = K.sum(b, axis=1)
```

The backend concept could be adapted in three ways (1a, 1b, 2):

1. Create an independent pybullet backend inspired by Keras backends with either:
    a. Immediate execution (standard python / numpy / pybullet style execution)
    b. Tensor graph based execution (tensorflow/theano/keras style execution)
2. Adopt and extend the actual current keras backend API for pybullet.

In addition to a backend, do consider tensor graphs for the performance benefits, debugging tools, and easy integration with TF & friends. :-)

issue_title: websocket server: sending right after prepare() doesn't work
issue_body:
## Long story short

A server that creates a websocket response.  If I send a message to the client as soon as the client connects, it doesn't work.  I have to wait for some message from the client before sending it data. 

Basically, sending messages only works inside the `async for msg in ws` loop.  Before it, it triggers a protocol error.

It sounds like there needs to be some hand-shaking between server and client before we can send messages to the client.  But I see no way to synchronise, to wait for this hand-shaking to happen.  My websocket protocol is supposed to send a bunch of messages to the client as soon as the client connects, without waiting for the client to send any request.

## Expected behaviour

I'd expect that, as soon as I connect, I'd receive a message:

```
> $ wscat -c 10.102.248.99:5000/v1/streamer?token=c3baacb76aa48bc32dd3a700cc5876d9
connected (press CTRL+C to quit)
< "xxxx"
```



## Actual behaviour

I get this error in wscat:

```
$ wscat -c 10.102.248.99:5000/v1/streamer?token=c3baacb76aa48bc32dd3a700cc5876d9
connected (press CTRL+C to quit)
error: Error: no handler for opcode 6
```

(with compression enabled, the invalid opcode changes to 12)

## Steps to reproduce
```python
from aiohttp import web
import aiohttp
import uvloop


async def web_stream(request: web.Request) -> web.Response:
    ws = web.WebSocketResponse(compress=False)
    await ws.prepare(request)
    await ws.send_json('xxxx')  # moving this statement to inside the the async for loop below "fixes" it
    async for msg in ws:
        print(msg)
        if msg.type == aiohttp.WSMsgType.TEXT:
            print(msg.data)
            if msg.data == 'close':
                await ws.close()
            else:
                await ws.send_json(msg.data + '/answer')
    return ws


def main() -> None:
    asyncio.set_event_loop_policy(uvloop.EventLoopPolicy())
    app = web.Application()
    app.router.add_get('/v1/streamer', web_stream)
    web.run_app(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=80)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

```

## Your environment

both

Server is uvloop, aiohttp 3.1.3, Python 3.6.5.
Client is wscat 1.1.0:
```
||/ Name                                                 Version                         Architecture                    Description
+++-====================================================-===============================-===============================-=============================================================================================================
ii  node-ws                                              1.1.0+ds1.e6ddaae4-3build1      amd64                           RFC-6455 WebSocket implementation module for Node.js
```

There is no proxy involved.

issue_title: SSL with closed connections

## Long story short

Receiving strange errors when bots are connecting to my SSL (HTTPS) server without sending any data and disconnecting afterwards.
SSL protocol handler inside aiohttp shows unexpected errors. This never happens to plain HTTP connections.

## Expected behaviour

No messages received (as for HTTP protocol).

task: <Task pending coro=<RequestHandler.start() done, defined at C:\Program Files\Python36\lib\site-packages\aiohttp\web_protocol.py:342> wait_for=<Future pending cb=[<TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x000001E0FBF59D98>()]>>

Run simple example:

```
import asyncio  
import aiohttp.web
import ssl

async def fetch_page():
    while True:
        reader, writer = await asyncio.open_connection('127.0.0.1', 443)

async def request_handler(request: aiohttp.web.BaseRequest):

if __name__ == '__main__':

    loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.gather(asyncio.Task(start_server())))
```

```python
async def middleware_cors(app, handler):
    access_control_allow_headers = ', '.join((
        hdrs.ORIGIN, hdrs.CONTENT_TYPE, hdrs.ACCEPT, hdrs.AUTHORIZATION, hdrs.WWW_AUTHENTICATE))
    access_control_methods = ', '.join((method for method in hdrs.METH_ALL if hasattr(handler, method.lower())))

    async def middleware_handler(request):
        if request.method == hdrs.METH_OPTIONS:
            response = web.Response()
        else:
            response = await handler(request)
        response.headers.update({
            hdrs.ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_ORIGIN: request.headers.get(hdrs.ORIGIN, '*'),
            hdrs.ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_HEADERS: access_control_allow_headers,
            hdrs.ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_METHODS: access_control_methods,
            hdrs.ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_CREDENTIALS: 'true',
        })
        return response
    return middleware_handler
```

And i want to get origins hosts from settings, etc

What do u think?

issue_title: Why I got ignored exception when I use aiomysql in python 3.5
issue_body:
Basic Info:

- python 3.5
- run the code with SublimeREPL

The exception traceback is as follows.

    Exception ignored in: <bound method Connection.__del__ of <aiomysql.connection.Connection object at 0x00000030F8080B38>>
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\site-packages\aiomysql\connection.py", line 689, in __del__
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\site-packages\aiomysql\connection.py", line 261, in close
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\asyncio\selector_events.py", line 569, in close
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\asyncio\base_events.py", line 447, in call_soon
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\asyncio\base_events.py", line 456, in _call_soon
      File "C:\software\development\python3.5\lib\asyncio\base_events.py", line 284, in _check_closed
    RuntimeError: Event loop is closed



I implement a simple ORM framework with some functions to process SQL.Some related codes in orm.py(just ignore the chinese annotation) as follows. The update or findAll method in class Model works well and really give the results correctly but every time after I running the test_method,it gives the exception.

    @asyncio.coroutine
    def create_pool(loop, **kw):		# 引入关键字后不用显示import asyncio了
        # 该函数用于创建连接池
        global __pool  # 全局变量用于保存连接池
        __pool = yield from aiomysql.create_pool(
            host=kw.get('host', 'localhost'),  # 默认定义host名字为localhost
            port=kw.get('port', 3306),		# 默认定义mysql的默认端口是3306
            user=kw['user'],				# user是通过关键字参数传进来的
            password=kw['password'],		# 密码也是通过关键字参数传进来的
            db=kw['db'],					# 数据库的名字
            charset=kw.get('charset', 'utf8'),  # 默认数据库字符集是utf8
            autocommit=kw.get('autocommit', True),  # 默认自动提交事务
            maxsize=kw.get('maxsize', 10),		# 连接池最多同时处理10个请求
            minsize=kw.get('minsize', 1),		# 连接池最少1个请求
            loop=loop		# 传递消息循环对象loop用于异步执行
        )
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def execute(sql, args, autocommit=True):
        # execute方法只返回结果数，不返回结果集,用于insert,update这些SQL语句
        log(sql)
        with (yield from __pool) as conn:
            if not autocommit:
                yield from conn.begin()
            try:
                cur = yield from conn.cursor()
                # 执行sql语句，同时替换占位符
                # pdb.set_trace()
                yield from cur.execute(sql.replace('?', '%s'), args)
                affected = cur.rowcount     # 返回受影响的行数
                yield from cur.close()       # 关闭游标
                if not autocommit:
                    yield from conn.commit()
            except BaseException as e:
                if not autocommit:
                    yield from conn.rollback()
                raise e  # raise不带参数，则把此处的错误往上抛;为了方便理解还是建议加e吧
            return affected


    class Model(dict, metaclass=ModelMetaclass):
        # 继承dict是为了使用方便，例如对象实例user['id']即可轻松通过UserModel去数据库获取到id
        # 元类自然是为了封装我们之前写的具体的SQL处理函数，从数据库获取数据
    
        def __init__(self, **kw):
            # 调用dict的父类__init__方法用于创建Model,super(类名，类对象)
            super(Model, self).__init__(**kw)
    
        def __getattr__(self, key):
            # 调用不存在的属性时返回一些内容
            try:
                return self[key]  # 如果存在则正常返回
            except KeyError:
                raise AttributeError(
                    r"'Model' object has no attribute '%s'" % key)		# r表示不转义
    
        def __setattr__(self, key, value):
            # 设定Model里面的key-value对象，这里value允许为None
            self[key] = value
    
        def getValue(self, key):
            # 获取某个具体的值，肯定存在的情况下使用该函数,否则会使用__getattr()__
            # 获取实例的key，None是默认值，getattr方法使用可以参考http://kaimingwan.com/post/python/pythonzhong-de-nei-zhi-han-shu-getattr-yu-fan-she
            return getattr(self, key, None)
    
        def getValueOrDefault(self, key):
            # 这个方法当value为None的时候能够返回默认值
            value = getattr(self, key, None)
            if value is None:		# 不存在这样的值则直接返回
                # self.__mapping__在metaclass中，用于保存不同实例属性在Model基类中的映射关系
                field = self.__mappings__[key]
                if field.default is not None:  # 如果实例的域存在默认值，则使用默认值
                    # field.default是callable的话则直接调用
                    value = field.default() if callable(field.default) else field.default
                    logging.debug('using default value for %s:%s' %
                                  (key, str(value)))
                    setattr(self, key, value)
            return value
    
    
    # --------------------------每个Model类的子类实例应该具备的执行SQL的方法比如save------
        @classmethod    # 类方法
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def findAll(cls, where=None, args=None, **kw):
            sql = [cls.__select__]  # 获取默认的select语句
            if where:   # 如果有where语句，则修改sql变量
                # 这里不用协程，是因为不需要等待数据返回
                sql.append('where')  # sql里面加上where关键字
                sql.append(where)   # 这里的where实际上是colName='xxx'这样的条件表达式
            if args is None:    # 什么参数?
                args = []
    
            orderBy = kw.get('orderBy', None)    # 从kw中查看是否有orderBy属性
            if orderBy:
                sql.append('order by')
                sql.append(orderBy)
    
            limit = kw.get('limit', None)    # mysql中可以使用limit关键字
            if limit is not None:
                sql.append('limit')
                if isinstance(limit, int):   # 如果是int类型则增加占位符
                    sql.append('?')
                    args.append(limit)
                elif isinstance(limit, tuple) and len(limit) == 2:   # limit可以取2个参数，表示一个范围
                    sql.append('?,?')
                    args.extend(limit)
                else:       # 其他情况自然是语法问题
                    raise ValueError('Invalid limit value: %s' % str(limit))
                # 在原来默认SQL语句后面再添加语句，要加个空格
    
            rs = yield from select(' '.join(sql), args)
            return [cls(**r) for r in rs]   # 返回结果，结果是list对象，里面的元素是dict类型的
    
        @classmethod
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def findNumber(cls, selectField, where=None, args=None):
            # 获取行数
            # 这里的 _num_ 什么意思？别名？ 我估计是mysql里面一个记录实时查询结果条数的变量
            sql = ['select %s _num_ from `%s`' % (selectField, cls.__table__)]
            # pdb.set_trace()
            if where:
                sql.append('where')
                sql.append(where)   # 这里不加空格？
            rs = yield from select(' '.join(sql), args, 1)  # size = 1
            if len(rs) == 0:  # 结果集为0的情况
                    return None
            return rs[0]['_num_']   # 有结果则rs这个list中第一个词典元素_num_这个key的value值
    
        @classmethod
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def find_by_key(cls, pk):
            # 根据主键查找
            # pk是dict对象
            rs = yield from select('%s where `%s`=?' % (cls.__select__, cls.__primary_key__), [pk], 1)
            if len(rs) == 0:
                return None
            return cls(**rs[0])
    
        # 这个是实例方法
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def save(self):
            # arg是保存所有Model实例属性和主键的list,使用getValueOrDefault方法的好处是保存默认值
            # 将自己的fields保存进去
            args = list(map(self.getValueOrDefault, self.__fields__))
            args.append(self.getValueOrDefault(self.__primary_key__))
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__insert__, args)  # 使用默认插入函数
            if rows != 1:
                # 插入失败就是rows!=1
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to insert record: affected rows: %s' % rows)
    
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def update(self):
            # 这里使用getValue说明只能更新那些已经存在的值，因此不能使用getValueOrDefault方法
            args = list(map(self.getValue, self.__fields__))
            args.append(self.getValue(self.__primary_key__))
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__update__, args)    # args是属性的list
            if rows != 1:
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to update by primary key: affected rows: %s' % rows)
    
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def remove(self):
            args = [self.getValue(self.__primary_key__)]
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__delete__, args)
            if rows != 1:
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to remove by primary key: affected rows: %s' % rows)

The test file is as follows.

    from models import User
    import asyncio
    import sys
    import orm
    import pdb
    import time
    
    # import pdb
    
    # 测试插入
    
    
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_save(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        u = User(name='hi', email='hi@example.com',
                 passwd='hi', image='about:blank')
        # pdb.set_trace()
        yield from u.save()
    
    # 测试查询
    
    
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_findAll(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        # 这里给的关键字参数按照xxx='xxx'的形式给出，会自动分装成dict
        rs = yield from User.findAll(email='test@example.com')		# rs是一个元素为dict的list
        # pdb.set_trace()
        for i in range(len(rs)):
            print(rs[i])
    
    # 查询条数?
    
    
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_findNumber(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        count = yield from User.findNumber('email')
        print(count)
    
    # 根据主键查找，这里试ID
    
    
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_find_by_key(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        # rs是一个dict
        # ID请自己通过数据库查询
        rs = yield from User.find_by_key('0014531826762080b29033a78624bc68c867550778f64d6000')
        print(rs)
    
    # 根据主键删除
    
    
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_remove(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        # 用id初始化一个实例对象
        u = User(id='0014531826762080b29033a78624bc68c867550778f64d6000')
        yield from u.remove()
    
    
    # 根据主键更新
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def test_update(loop):
        yield from orm.create_pool(loop, user='kami', password='kami', db='pure_blog')
        # 必须按照列的顺序来初始化：'update `users` set `created_at`=?, `passwd`=?, `image`=?,
        # `admin`=?, `name`=?, `email`=? where `id`=?' 注意这里要使用time()方法，否则会直接返回个时间戳对象，而不是float值
        u = User(id='00145318300622886f186530ee74afabecedb42f9cd590a000', created_at=time.time(), passwd='test',
                 image='about:blank', admin=True, name='test', email='hello1@example.com')  # id必须和数据库一直，其他属性可以设置成新的值,属性要全
        # pdb.set_trace()
        yield from u.update()
    
    
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()  # 获取消息循环对象
    loop.run_until_complete(test_update(loop))  # 执行协程
    loop.close()

issue_title: Release 0.0.4
issue_body:
CHANGES
--------------
* Allow to call connection.wait_closed twice.
* Fixed sqlalchemy 1.0.0 support.
* Fix #11: Rename Connection.wait_closed() to .ensure_closed()
* Raise ResourceWarning on non-closed Connection
* Rename Connection.connect to _connect

issue_title: [Linux] Cache files in $XDG_CACHE_HOME/tldr instead of ~/.tldr_cache
issue_body:
According to the [XDG Base Directory Specification](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-0.8.html), user-specific cached files files should be stored in `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/tldr`, or `$HOME/.cache/tldr` if `$XDG_CACHE_HOME` is not defined.

> There is a single base directory relative to which user-specific non-essential (cached) data should be written. This directory is defined by the environment variable $XDG_CACHE_HOME. 

This avoids cluttering the home directory and simplifies including/excluding related directories from bulk operations such as [backups](https://github.com/puniverse/capsule/issues/104).

### Links
- [XDG Base Directory support - Arch Wiki](https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/XDG_Base_Directory_support)
- [XDG_CACHE_HOME equivalent on Mac OS X - Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/a/5084892)

### Repos
- [jleclanche/python-xdg](https://github.com/jleclanche/python-xdg)
- [wijnen/python-fhs](https://github.com/wijnen/python-fhs)

issue_title: Remove **kwargs if not used
issue_body:
I had fllowing situation:

```python
from schematics.models import Model
from schematics.types import IntType

class Mapping(Model):
    value = IntType(deserialized_from='@value')

assert Mapping({'@value': '1'}, strict=False).to_primitive() == {'value': 1}
# => {'value': None} != {'value': 1}
```

Here I misspelled `deserialized_from` argument name and it took quite some time to figure it out, because I did not suspected, that `IntType` can swallow arguments with any name.

This happened, because `BaseType` accepts `**kwargs` without using it, which allows all type classes to accept any arguments.

If not used, `**kwargs` should be removed from `BaseType` constructor letting Python do automatic passed argument check.

After removing `**kwargs` I will get nice Python error instead of silence:

```pytb
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'deserialized_from'
```

issue_title: add upgrade instructions to readme.md
issue_body: Noticed that we do not have instructions for upgrading `blockstack-core` in the README.md.

issue_title: API: return structured zone files, or error message where appropriate
issue_body: There are a few cases where we return a raw zone file as part of an aggregate JSON object, such as name whois info.  This should not be allowed--either a well-formed zone file may be returned (i.e. one that is RFC-compliant), or an error message should be returned.  The reason is that non-compliant zone files may be arbitrary byte strings, which is not compatible with JSON.

issue_title: Combined NAME_REGISTER/NAME_UPDATE operation
issue_body: Offer a new operation that combines NAME_REGISTER and NAME_UPDATE, to avoid wasting an extra transaction.

issue_title: API: /v1/names should return subdomains
issue_body: Multiple API consumers (such as search) rely on /v1/names to return all names.  This should include subdomains; otherwise apps that rely on search won't work with them.

issue_title: Use Blueprint for integrating search
issue_body:
I recently converted the [resolver](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/api/api/resolver.py) interface to be used as a Blueprint [in the Flask API](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/api/api/__init__.py).

And now the following (legacy) endpoint work in the Blockstack API:

> /v2/users/muneeb

We need to do the same for the [search subsystem](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/tree/api/api/search). The most important endpoint to support/test is: 

> /search?query=wenger

Demo at: https://search.blockstack.org/search?query=wenger

Our goal is to retire both resolver.onename.com and search.blockstack.org eventually and
support the (legacy) resolver and search functionality at http://core.blockstack.org

issue_title: Atlas: support auto-ban for misbehaving peers
issue_body:
Expand the Atlas peer-processing logic to detect when a peer repeatedly announces invalid data, such as:

* invalid zone file inventory vectors
* stale or invalid consensus hashes
* old versions

When such a peer is detected, ban them for ~24 hours.  Remove them completely from the peer table.

issue_title: Interactive mode doesn't kill API endpoint on shutdown
issue_body:
When you run blockstack integration tests in interactive mode (which is how we test and develop on browser, etc.), sending a Ctrl-C SIGINT to the process *usually* shuts down the blockstackd process and bitcoind, but the API endpoint is almost always still alive:

```
$ blockstack-test-scenario --interactive 2 blockstack_integration_tests.scenarios.portal_test_env 2>&1 | tee /tmp/stdout | grep "inish"
...
Test finished; doing checks
^C
```
```
$ ps aux | grep blockstack-test
aaron    19669  3.4  0.4 1019232 66712 ?       Sl   13:49   0:31 /home/aaron/devel/blockstack-core.2/.venv/bin/python2 /home/aaron/devel/blockstack-core/.venv/bin/blockstack-test-scenario --interactive 2 blockstack_integration_tests.scenarios.portal_test_env
```

This is a mild annoyance, but a persistent one that I hope to get around to squashing sooner rather than later.

issue_title: Slicing Indexes
issue_body:
We have run up against an issue with the lazy loading of images with `filestore`. At CSX we usualy take datasets which are of a large number of images per point. As we index our data as :
```
[(point), (image), (y), (X)]
```
We lazy load on point. i.e. the generator goes through and returns `[(image), (y), (x)]`.

The problem with this, is this breaks if you have `(image)` >> `(point)`.

How do we lazy load 2 deep?

issue_title: Regression in 0.15 with I202 (newline in imports)
issue_body:
For a given file `repro.py`, there is a regression:

```
$ virtualenv venv
$ venv/bin/pip install flake8 flake8-import-order numpy
$ venv/bin/python repro.py
Here are my imports:
- matplotlib.pyplot: None
- numpy: <module 'numpy' from '.../venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/numpy/__init__.py'>
- seaborn: None
- six: <module 'six' from '.../venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/six.py'>
$
$ venv/bin/pip show flake8-import-order
Name: flake8-import-order
Version: 0.15
$ venv/bin/flake8 repro.py --import-order-style=google
repro.py:10:1: I202 Additional newline in a section of imports.
$
$ venv/bin/pip install 'flake8-import-order==0.14.3'
$ venv/bin/flake8 repro.py --import-order-style=google
$
```

----

```python
# repro.py
try:
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
except ImportError:
    plt = None
import numpy as np
try:
    import seaborn  # pylint: disable=unused-import
except ImportError:
    seaborn = None
import six


def main():
    """This is the entry point."""
    print('Here are my imports:')
    print('- matplotlib.pyplot: {}'.format(plt))
    print('- numpy: {}'.format(np))
    print('- seaborn: {}'.format(seaborn))
    print('- six: {}'.format(six))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

I don't have any preference for how I do my imports, so I'm happy to change the imports to "conform" to what is expected. Are there equivalent docs? My logic was "keep them in alphabetical order and catch the potential `ImportError`-s as they come".

I have ["fixed"][1] this issue for now in a real code base just by ignoring the error.

[1]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/4400e4f300a0b227d7610a3f20d711ea6fe0e0d9/tests/unit/test__algebraic_intersection.py#L21

issue_title: Issue with resource file string entry, string decode size calculation
issue_body:
I've come across the following issues with regards to Androguard/core/bytecodes/apk.py's `StringBlock `class when retrieving an entry from a string pool which is UTF8 encoded. 

Android does not appear to enforce/check the overflow of the string size fields in a String Pool Entry represented in `decode8` and `decode16` as `str_len` and `encoded_bytes`. It is therefore possible in a UTF8 encoded string for the `encoded_bytes` value to overflow the 0x7FFF (2 byte encoding) maximum size in this encoding. This doesn't seem to appear with UTF16 encoded strings, however this may just be due to the size of string required to overflow!

The net effect is this  leads to incorrect (truncated) string from the StringBlock entry retrieval methods and Androguard raising a warning.

Basically the `encoded_bytes` entry now becomes unreliable and should not be used in the `decode8` or `decode16` to slice the string. Since both UTF8 and UTF16 string entries should always be zero terminated I would propose the following to resolve the string retrieval issue (this is also how Android does it):

```
     def decode8(self, offset):
        temp = ResStringEntryUTF8.parse(self.m_charbuff[offset:])
        str_len, skip = self.decodeLength(offset, 1)
        offset += skip

        encoded_bytes, skip = self.decodeLength(offset, 1)
        offset += skip

        str_end = self.m_charbuff[offset: ].find('\x00')
        data = self.m_charbuff[offset: offset + str_end]

        return self.decode_bytes(data, 'utf-8', str_len)

```
```

    def decode16(self, offset):
        temp = ResStringEntryUTF16.parse(self.m_charbuff[offset:])
        str_len, skip = self.decodeLength(offset, 2)
        offset += skip

        encoded_bytes = str_len * 2

        str_end = self.m_charbuff[offset: ].find('\x00\x00')
        data = self.m_charbuff[offset: offset + str_end]

        return self.decode_bytes(data, 'utf-16', str_len)
```
I can't come up with a clean way around the size check, but that's not such a problem if the warning is updated to generate a specific message for this issue (For example: "Invalid decoded string length:: String larger than max string size encoding limit"). 

The problem can be reproduced using  NBA JAM ver 40008.

issue_title: 0.5.0 release!
Drop __multicall__ support
issue_body:
As per the comment in [`pluggy._MultiCall`](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy.py#L586) we should be able to drop the line [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy.py#L613). Grepping the `pytest` sources reveals that only a [few spots still reference it](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/search?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=__multicall__).

This change also allows us to remove the recursion induced by [`_wrapped_call()` getting passed `_MultiCall.execute`](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/pluggy.py#L613). This means we can remove `_wrapped_call` and simply loop through hookwrapper pre and post `yield` calls at the beginning and end of `execute()`. I'd actually be interested to see if `contextlib.contextmanager` wrapping could be used directly for this.

issue_title: goog:chromeOptions from capabilities json are ignored when remote driver is used
issue_body:
I want to run my tests using a remote driver in headless chrome browser. I'm using pytest-variables to pass capabilities.json as capabilities for the driver but the browser is not being run in headless mode (chrome window is opened every time). 

I think the json is in a correct format as it is working when I use json with selenium driver directly. That's why I started suspecting that  the issue is in pytest-selenium. 

While I was going through the code I found that in "capabilities" fixture inside pytest_selenium/pytest_selenium.py before the block handling remote driver class(from line 60 to line 72) the goog:chromeOptions are in the capabilities variable but after the block they are removed  from capabilities. 

Im trying to understand the "if" block on pytest_selenium/pytest_selenium.py:70: 

`        if all([key, options]):
            capabilities.setdefault(key, {}).update(options.get(key, {}))`

for example when CHROME is the browser both key and options are set resulting in all([key, options]) returning "True" and thus executing the capabilities.setdefault... What I don't understand is what is it supposed to do? Right now it rewrites the capabilities that are loaded from the json with defaults. (as we see in the "capabilities" after the block). Shouldn't it work the other way around and combine the default values with the capabilities provided in the json? Am I missing something or is there something wrong in my setup? 


**Run command:**
`polka$ pytest ./tests/test_login.py --driver=Remote --variables=./capabilities_chrome.json`

**Platform and pytest plugins:**
- platform darwin -- Python 3.6.1, pytest-3.4.1, py-1.5.2, pluggy-0.6.0
- plugins: xdist-1.22.2, variables-1.7.1, selenium-1.11.4, metadata-1.6.0, html-1.16.1, forked-0.2, base-url-1.4.1

**Contents of capabilities_chrome.json:**
`{
  "capabilities": {
    "browserName": "chrome",
    "platform": "Mac",
    "goog:chromeOptions": {
      "args": [
        "headless"
      ]
    }
  }
}`


**Before the block, capabilities are:**
`"{'browserName': 'chrome', 'platform': 'Mac', 'goog:chromeOptions': {'args': ['headless']}}"`

**After the block, capabilities are:** 
`"{'browserName': 'chrome', 'platform': 'Mac', 'goog:chromeOptions': {'args': [], 'extensions': []}}"`

issue_title: Troubles to set up internal NuGet server for .NET Core development/publishing
issue_body:
A company can block internet access for its internal development machines. In such cases, an internal NuGet server would be required to host the necessary packages, so as to enable .NET Core development and publishing.

I built a detailed example and documented it in [my blog post](https://blog.lextudio.com/offline-publishing-asp-net-core-apps-cf22f45158af), which shows how troublesome the experience is today, when attempting to publish the default ASP.NET Core MVC project. 

To summarize,

* The default NuGet source setting specifies nuget.org, which if blocked leads to an error. This error can only be resolved by adding a custom nuget.config file, which is not pleasant.
* `dotnet publish -r win-x64` would generate errors on missing packages, which is good, but there is no easy way to extract a list of NuGet packages (with version numbers) out and download them. Ideally, such can be done by a PowerShell script, but no sample anyway.
* The `Detected package downgrade` error message can be misleading, while the fix is to explicitly set `<RuntimeFrameworkVersion>`.

Hope there can be official guides from Microsoft to show how such internal NuGet server should be set up (especially which packages are mandatory). That can save tons of hours for those companies to explore on their own.

issue_title: letsencrypt-auto --help should not run "apt-get install -y ..." automatically
issue_body:
I just had a machine trashed.

Running `./letsencrypt-auto --help` ran `apt-get install -y ...` automatically, which, among other things updated `libc`, trashing the entire machine to a point where re-installation was the easiest path. Sure the cause was a misconfiguration of `/etc/apt/sources` performed by us months ago, but still, `./letsencrypt-auto --help` shouldn't trash the machine because of that.

I suggest removing the `-y` switch so that the user is prompted for confirmation before apt-get does its thing, and/or a `letsencrypt-auto --yes-to-apt` switch or something. If one were to argue that the '-auto' in `letsencrypt-auto` implies automatic behaviour, then please provide a `letsencrypt-non-auto` or something *and use __that__ as the default in the documentation*.

Please don't perform an operation that can trash my entire machine by running `./letsencrypt-auto --help`. Nobody expects that. 

Or at the very least check that this doesn't install any packages you didn't anticipate. Perhaps running `apt-get install -s $packages` and testing the output. If only packages from an expected white-list show up, then run `apt-get install -y $packages` otherwise omit the -y and do `apt-get install $packages`, so the user is prompted for confirmation.

issue_title: run_cmd: Popen.communicate doesn't take a string
issue_body:
`powerline.lib.shell.run_cmd` function calls `Popen.communicate(stdin)`.
`powerline.lib.shell.asrun` function calls `run_cmd` and passes `ascript` to its 3rd argument (`stdin`).
`powerline.segments.common.spotify` calls `asrun` with an apple script of type "string".

But according to Python [2.7](https://docs.python.org/2/library/subprocess.html) and [3.5](https://docs.python.org/3/library/subprocess.html) docs, `Popen.communicate` takes a "data", not a "string".

So as a result, `spotify_apple_script` doesn't work and complain about expecting bytes but receiving str, throws an error.

issue_title: Add option to sync command to ignore modification time.
issue_body:
I have a use case that sounds pretty common. I am generating a set of files that have a hash in their file name so that any time the files have the same name they will have the same content. Think of rails asset pipeline where a hash is inserted into the file name. I then want to upload all new files to b2 so that they can be used.

The sync command is almost perfect as this except that even if the files have the same name and contents they will be reuploaded because the modification time is newer. It would be nice to have an option that would ignore the modification time and just use the name (and possibly the size).

(Bonus points: add an option to get an error if the size is different rather then reuploading or skipping)

This is similar to rsync's `--ignore-existing` which only copies new files.

issue_title: Consistent Default Text Delimiters
issue_body:
Different functions in `turicreate.text_analytics` use different default values for delimiters.

`count_words` `trim_rare_words` `tokenize` uses:
["\r", "\v", "\n", "\f", "\t", " "]

`count_ngrams` uses:
["\r", "\v", "\n", "\f", "\t", " ", "!", "#", "$", "%", "&", "'", "(", ")",
   "*", "+", ",", "-", ".", "/", ":", ";", "<", "=", ">", "?", "@", "[", "\\", "]",
   "^", "_", "`", "{", "|", "}", "~"]

I think we should always use the longer list.

In addition we should add the double quote character (ie`"`) to list, single quote character is already included.

issue_title: Replace libiomp.so soft link with a configurable path in native project
issue_body:
When build native project, libiomp.so will be added into the jar package. Currently, the libiomp.so is checkin to the code base with a soft link point to /opt/intel/... This is bad, because 1. there will be no error if intel mkl is not installed into that path. The error can't find libiomp.so will be thrown in runtime; 2. the path can't be configurable.

So I think we should use a configurable path(stored in env var or java property) to replace the soft link file. The default value can be /opt/intel/..., but it will throw error in compile time when can't find the so file and user can modify that value when the intel mkl is installed in another place in their box.

issue_title: SpatialBatchNormalization does not work while affine is false
issue_body:
In BatchNormalization, weight initialization is based on `nOutput` and `affine`
```scala
 val weight: Tensor[T] =
    if (initWeight != null) initWeight else if (affine) Tensor[T](nOutput) else null
```
Weight will be null if affine is false, but it's not handled properly in forward and backward.

issue_title: [82] syntax error on escaped quotes in double quoted strings
issue_body:
**Reported by cdur...@gmail.com, 2013-05-01T05:34:13Z**

According to http://www.graphviz.org/doc/info/lang.html, an ID can be:
...
'any double-quoted string ("...") possibly containing escaped quotes (\")1;'

pydot does not properly read double quoted strings with escaped quotes

##### What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. pydot.graph_from_dot_data("graph G { a [test=\"\\\"escaped quotes\\\"\"] }")

or

1) create the following DOT file:
```
graph G {
  a [test="\"escaped quotes\""]
}
```
2) pydot.graph_from_dot_file("file.dot")

##### What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
expected output: <pydot.Dot object at 0xb483de8c>
output: 
```
graph G { a [test="\"escaped quotes\""] }
            ^
Expected "}" (at char 12), (line:1, col:13)
```
##### What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
pydot 1.0.28; Python 2.7.3; Gentoo/Linux 3.0.6

##### Please provide any additional information below.
A suggested patch is attached. Apply to dot_parser.py
patch -Np1 dot_parser.py escChar.patch
Attached [**`escChar.patch`**](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/anonymous/594ae367f91ace739f1c/raw/50edf04648a1e26f28b6b15d1dc5ab6e93ea5304/escChar.patch) ([view on Gist](https://gist.github.com/594ae367f91ace739f1c))
From: https://code.google.com/p/pydot/issues/detail?id=82

issue_title: understanding config scope
issue_body:
I tried modifying the modular example to download my dataset:

```pyton
#!/usr/bin/env python
# coding=utf-8
"""
This is a script for downloading and converting the pascal voc 2012 dataset
and the berkely extended version.

    # original PASCAL VOC 2012
    # wget http://host.robots.ox.ac.uk/pascal/VOC/voc2012/VOCtrainval_11-May-2012.tar # 2 GB

    # berkeley augmented Pascal VOC
    # wget http://www.eecs.berkeley.edu/Research/Projects/CS/vision/grouping/semantic_contours/benchmark.tgz # 1.3 GB

This can be run as an independent executable to download
the dataset or be imported by scripts used for larger experiments.

If you aren't sure run this to do a full download + conversion setup of the dataset:
   ./data_pascal_voc.py pascal_voc_setup
"""
from __future__ import division, print_function, unicode_literals
from sacred import Ingredient, Experiment
import sys
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
from collections import defaultdict
import os
from keras.utils import get_file
# from tf_image_segmentation.recipes import datasets
from tf_image_segmentation.utils.tf_records import write_image_annotation_pairs_to_tfrecord
from tf_image_segmentation.utils import pascal_voc
from os.path import expanduser

# ============== Ingredient 0: settings =================
s = Ingredient("settings")


@s.config
def cfg1():
    verbose = True


# ============== Ingredient 1: dataset.paths =================
data_paths = Ingredient("dataset.paths", ingredients=[s])


@data_paths.config
def cfg2(settings):
    v = not settings['verbose']
    base = expanduser("~") + "/datasets"


# ============== Ingredient 2: dataset =======================
data_pascal_voc = Experiment("dataset", ingredients=[data_paths, s])


@data_pascal_voc.config
def cfg3(paths):
    dataset_path = paths['base'] + '/VOC2012'
    # sys.path.append("tf-image-segmentation/")
    # os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"] = '1'
    # based on https://github.com/martinkersner/train-DeepLab

    # original PASCAL VOC 2012
    # wget http://host.robots.ox.ac.uk/pascal/VOC/voc2012/VOCtrainval_11-May-2012.tar # 2 GB
    pascal_root = dataset_path + '/VOCdevkit/VOC2012'

    # berkeley augmented Pascal VOC
    # wget http://www.eecs.berkeley.edu/Research/Projects/CS/vision/grouping/semantic_contours/benchmark.tgz # 1.3 GB
    pascal_berkeley_root = dataset_path + '/benchmark_RELEASE'
    urls = [
        'http://host.robots.ox.ac.uk/pascal/VOC/voc2012/VOCtrainval_11-May-2012.tar',
        'http://www.eecs.berkeley.edu/Research/Projects/CS/vision/grouping/semantic_contours/benchmark.tgz'
    ]
    filenames = [
        'VOCtrainval_11-May-2012.tar',
        'benchmark.tgz'
    ]


@data_pascal_voc.capture
def pascal_voc_files(dataset_path, filenames, paths, settings, urls):
    print(dataset_path)
    print(paths)
    print(settings)
    return [dataset_path + filename for filename in filenames]


@data_pascal_voc.command
def pascal_voc_download(dataset_path, filenames, paths, settings, urls):
    zip_paths = pascal_voc_files(dataset_path, filenames, paths, settings, urls)
    for url, filename in zip(urls, filenames):
        get_file(filename, url, untar=True, cache_subdir=dataset_path)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Create pascal voc experiment so dataset interaction
    # can be run as its own executable
    # ex = Experiment('pascalvoc', ingredients=[data_pascal_voc, data_paths])
    data_pascal_voc.run_commandline()
```

issue_title: codecov shows develop by default
issue_body:
We recently switched GW to using master but codecov still thinks that develop is the default. Therefore, our badge and the [codecov GW landing page](https://codecov.io/gh/Becksteinlab/GromacsWrapper/) show the outdated develop branch.

Instead it should represent the [codecov master](https://codecov.io/gh/Becksteinlab/GromacsWrapper/branch/master).

Supposedly, [Codecov uses the repository default branch](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/codecov-yaml#section-master-copy) but it seems it does not notice when I change the default branch in GitHub.

It is not obvious from that error what the problem is.

```

```

The strange thing is that
```

```
works fine, and my-cool-stack-name has no quotes here.

## Expected behavior:
No error when using
```
Value: {{SenzaInfo.StackName}}
```

or at least a more helpful message like:
```
Error parsing template file my_senza_template.yml
Found unhashable key line 174, column 19:
              Value: {{SenzaInfo.StackName}}
                      ^
Senza variables should be double quoted, ex: "{{SenzaInfo.StackName}}"
```

I got bitten by this issue enough times to report a bug.

issue_title: C++ api linking error due to missing inline in z3++.h
Java API equals() method of class "FuncDecl"
issue_body:
I find that in the Java API, each time the method getFuncDecl() in class Expr is called, a new instance of FuncDecl will be created and returned. However, the equals() method in FuncDecl basically returns the result of "this==o". As a result, two FuncDecl instances will be "not equal", even if they refer to the same function declaration. I guess it would be more reasonable if the equals() method returns "(this.getId()==((FuncDecl)o).getId)". Moreover, the hashCode() method might also need to be modified.

The class Symbol has a similar problem.
