issue_title: alternative pattern annotations for forall quantifiers are sometimes ignored by Z3
issue_body:
When a forall quantifier is provided with multiple alternative patterns, it seems that Z3 sometimes doesn't use anything but the first pattern. The behaviour seems quite sensitive to the kind of example, but I've managed to cut down one to a fairly reasonable Boogie input (and corresponding smt file). 

Here is the Boogie input:
[Z3_repro.bpl.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/35442/Z3_repro.bpl.txt)

The axiom starting on line 31 exhibits the problematic behaviour. Uncommenting the alternative version of this axiom below it (or just removing its first trigger annotation in this particular example) will make the file verify. Similarly, one can remove the first pattern annotation from the corresponding quantifier in the smt file, at line 184:
[triggering_repro.smt2.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/35441/triggering_repro.smt2.txt)

This could be related to the following (old) stack overflow question, where Leonardo confirmed and fixed a bug with [very similar symptoms.](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/11649305/triggering-problems-in-z3)

issue_title: Quantifier no longer instantiated if conjunct is added
issue_body:
The following program is expected to be yield `unsat`, and does. However, uncommenting the additional conjunct in the quantifier AX-1 changes the result to `unknown`.

```
(set-option :auto_config false)
(set-option :smt.mbqi false)

(declare-fun foo (Int) Bool)
(declare-const k Real)
(declare-fun p (Int) Bool)

(assert (forall ((i Int)) (!
  (and
    (< 0.0 k)
;    (p i) ;;; ---- uncomment this line ----
  )
  :pattern ((foo i))
  :qid |AX-1|)))

(assert (forall ((i Int)) (!
  (foo i)
  :pattern ((foo i))
  :qid |AX-2|)))

(declare-const j Int)
(assert (< j 0))

; (push) ;;; doesn't make a difference
(assert (not
  (ite
    (foo j)
    (< 0.0 k)
    false)))

; (set-option :smt.qi.profile true)
(check-sat)
; (get-info :all-statistics)
; (pop)
```

The quantifier instantiation statistics show that `AX-2` is instantiated in both cases, but `AX-1` is only instantiated if the additional conjunct is not included. My assumption is that in the latter case, Z3 eliminates the quantifier since the quantified variable doesn't occur in the body.

However, I find it surprising that the version with the additional conjunct - in which Z3 presumably doesn't eliminate the quantifier - yields unknown, since the trigger for the quantifier (`(foo j)`) should be available.

issue_title: Consider using PhantomReference instead of finalizers for Java interface
issue_body:
Hi All,

Z3 uses the `finalize()` method to add the objects to the queue, on which `dec_ref` needs to be eventually called.
While using finalizers feels natural, in our experience (while developing CPAchecker), we have found them to cause rather severe performance issues, both with time and memory (those are outlined in Effective Java, also a relevant article is http://resources.ej-technologies.com/jprofiler/help/doc/index.html).
In addition, because finalizers run on the other thread, such approach requires locking.

We have found that it is much more safe to use `PhantomReference` instead: a special reference, which does not increase the reference count of the object for GC purposes, but gets added to the queue once the referenced object is released by the GC.
An example of such usage is https://github.com/dbeyer/cpachecker/blob/trunk/src/org/sosy_lab/cpachecker/util/predicates/bdd/JavaBDDRegionManager.java: note the `ReferenceQueue`, and the map used to retrieve the stored pointer.
The `#poll` method is used on the map, which does not lock until the data is available (it does lock then, but for a very short period of time).

What would be your thoughts on the proposal?
I can do the pull request for the migration.

We currently do not have benchmarks data for phantom references vs. finalizers for Z3 specifically, but it did help tremendously in similar circumstances (native objects which required explicit free-ing).

issue_title: z3.parse_smt2_string() in python API fails when string contains UTF-8 characters
issue_body:
From SMT-LIBv2.6 UTF-8 can be used in `.smt2` files. The python API `z3.parse_smt2_string()` fails to work on these files because it assumes the string it is provided with must be ascii.

Here's how to reproduce

Here's the contents of `test.py`

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
import io
import logging
import sys
import z3

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def parse(query_file):
    assert isinstance(query_file, io.TextIOWrapper)
    # Load query_file as string
    query_str = ""
    for l in query_file.readlines():
        query_str += str(l)
    _logger.debug('query:\n{}'.format(query_str))
    try:
        constraint = z3.parse_smt2_string(query_str)
    except z3.z3types.Z3Exception as e:
        msg = 'Parsing failed: {}'.format(e)
        return (None, msg)
    return (constraint, None)

def main(args):
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
    file_name = args[0]
    with open(file_name, 'r') as f:
        contraint, msg = parse(f)
    return 0
        

if __name__ == '__main__':

issue_title: Cross mini-batch gradients average
issue_body:
GPU RAM are always rare resources. 

Although momentum act as some kind of cross mini-batch gradients average, I learned that it is not good enough, Do you like some updates support mini-batch gradients ?

I try to write some code like this:

    def apply_momentum(updates, params=None, momentum=0.9, average=5):

        N=average

        if params is None:
            params = updates.keys()
        updates = OrderedDict(updates)

        for param in params:
            value = param.get_value(borrow=True)
            sumval = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                     broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
            velocity = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                     broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
            count = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype='int8'),
                                     broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
            updates[count]=(count+1) % N

            avg=(sumval+updates[param])/N

            x = momentum * velocity + avg

            updates[sumval] = T.switch(T.eq(count,N-1),T.zeros_like(sumval),sumval+updates[param])
            updates[velocity] = T.switch(T.eq(count,N-1),x - param,velocity)
            updates[param] = T.switch(T.eq(count,N-1),x,param)

        return updates

issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```python
# Your code here
from satpy.scene import Scene
from satpy.resample import get_area_def
from datetime import datetime
from glob import glob

FILES = glob("/home/griffith/GIS/testData/*DAT")

scn = Scene(sensor='ahi',start_time=datetime(2017, 6, 23, 2, 50),end_time=datetime(2017, 6, 23, 3, 00),filenames=FILES,reader='ahi_hsd')
rgbname = 'true_color_reducedsize'
#scn.load([rgbname])
#scn.show(rgbname)

print(scn.available_dataset_names())
scn.load(["B01"])
local_scene = scn.resample("("eurol")

issue_body:
So I'm looking at this package as a way to manage my schema changes. Seems to work quite well, until a migration fails, at which point it's unclear how to recover.

I deliberately created a migration that was intended to fail: it contained a list of 10 ALTER statements, one of which specified a table that does not exist. cassandra-migrate correctly failed to apply the migration:

I then changed the v3_US2704_Compaction_Strategies.cql and removed the call to ALTER the unconfigured table and attempted to re-run:

issue_title: Restore gangamon server
issue_body:
OK, I need to get around to restoring the gangamon server in order to restore the `report` functionality.
I'm going to list the stages which need looking into here

- [ ] Update the code on github with the modified code from the current collection server to Centos7 compatible. (systemd service+new Django+new crontask+new Apache2 config etc.)
- [ ] Launch a new machine monitored and updates via Jenkins and Foreman
- [ ] Write some new Jenkins rules to pull the code from github, check the secrets from storage then update the machine.
- [ ] Make sure the machine can be recreated from scratch using this recipe.
- [ ] Writeup how it currently works and how the pieces fit together
- [ ] Change the DNS target which could prove difficult if the rule points to no machine?
- [ ] Get the machine released from behind the CERN firewall (ports 443 and 80 are all that's required I think).

After this has been done I'll look into fixing/expanding the `report` functionality and we can consider adding some more information to the stomp reporting.
(The current implementation of the listener may require scrapping eventually and being replaced with a more reliable python script with direct SQL commands. Although the DJango saves from a lot of this it doesn't scale very well in it's current form at all.)

issue_title: Problem with configuration system
issue_body:
A bug has been introduced in the configuration system which causes out-of-date values to be locked into a users `.gangarc` file. Two instances of this are
   * Starting Ganga as ganga -o'[Logging]Ganga.Lib=DEBUG' will if it at the same time is a release that the user has not used before
   * Values that are changed in `.INI` file are likewise locked into the users .gangarc when the `,gangarc` file is recreated with a new release.

As an example, my `.gangarc` file at Imperial has the values below uncommented

```
load_default_Dirac_backend = False
splitFilesChunks = 5000
datasets = string_dataset_shortcut
associate = {'log': 'TextMerger', 'sim': 'LHCbFileMerger', 'text': 'TextMerger', 'dst': 'LHCbFileMerger', 'txt': 'TextMerger', 'root': 'RootMerger', 'mdst': 'LHCbFileMerger', 'digi': 'LHCbFileMerger'}
creds_poll_rate = 86400
arch = x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt
location = /cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/RootConfig
path = /cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/RootConfig/pro/x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt
pythonhome = /cvmfs/lhcb.cern.ch/lib/GangaConfig/python/pro/x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt
pythonversion = pro
version = pro
os.environ["PATH"]+=":."
preexecute = exec('import os\nos.chdir(os.environ["TMPDIR"])\nos.environ["PATH"]+=":."\nos.environ["HOME"]=os.environ["TMPDIR"]\nimport shutil,pwd\nshutil.copy(os.path.join("/vols/lhcbdisk05",pwd.getpwuid(os.getuid())[0],".proxy","proxy"),"/tmp/x509up_u%d" % os.getuid())')
submit_str = /home/hep/lhcb/bin/batchproxy; cd %s; qsub -S /usr/bin/python -cwd -V %s %s %s %s
encodeDefaultProxyFileName = False
group = lhcb_user
options = []
submit_with_threads = False
pre_script = ['source /afs/cern.ch/lhcb/software/releases/LBSCRIPTS/prod/InstallArea/scripts/LbLogin.sh', 'source `runpy LbConfiguration.SetupProject --shell=sh --mktemp Ganga`']
queue = hepshort.q
```

The code should be made such that the only uncommented lines written into a new `.gangarc` are those that were uncommented in the file already. A method for cleaning up the mess created in users' `.gangarc` files should also be developed.

issue_title: Local wrapper script exiting with code 9
issue_body:
We've seen a few sporadic failures of test [Savannah19123](https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/blob/2939a9d04bac16cf9f6a25b90dbc3feb8cfc4d9f/python/Ganga/new_tests/GPI/Bugs/TestSavannah19123.py). The symptom is that the test is checking whether certain file exist or don't exist at the right times and at the end of the test it calls `j.kill()` which errors since the job is in the `failed` state (which it should not be). We couldn't work out why the job was failing so Mark added some debug to it. The debug hasn't helped directly but it's now occurred twice in a row on the Nightly tests.

You can see the output at [ganga-ci](https://ganga-ci.cern.ch/jenkins/job/Nightly/208/consoleText) but the important part is the logging output which has:
```
Ganga.Lib.Localhost : CRITICAL wrapper script for job 0 exit with code 9
Ganga.Lib.Localhost : CRITICAL report this as a bug at https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/issues/
```
so it seems that `__jobscript__` is failing in some way and is returning a `9` (whatever that means). I've seen this test fail on my local computer before so I don't think it's directly related to the canga-ci machine.

issue_title: broken doctests
issue_body:
There are a few things in the recently merged PRs that have broken some existing doctests.  (These can be run by `make doctest`  from within the `doc` folder.  These tests need to either be updated or removed.  Some are trivial to fix, but a few concern me:

One that fails:
```python
from math import sqrt
my_filter_bank = ([sqrt(2)/2, sqrt(2)/2], [-sqrt(2)/2, sqrt(2)/2],
                      [sqrt(2)/2, sqrt(2)/2], [sqrt(2)/2, -sqrt(2)/2])
my_wavelet = pywt.Wavelet('My Haar Wavelet', filter_bank=my_filter_bank)
print(my_wavelet)
```
```
Expected:
    Wavelet My Haar Wavelet
      Family name:
      Short name:
      Filters length: 2
      Orthogonal:     True
      Biorthogonal:   True
      Symmetry:       unknown
Got:
    Wavelet My Haar Wavelet
      Family name:    ]
      Short name:     I
      Filters length: 2
      Orthogonal:     True
      Biorthogonal:   True
      Symmetry:       unknown
      DWT:            True
      CWT:            False
```
The new DWT and CWT fields are to be expected, but why are characters displayed for "Family Name", "Short Name"?  I tried running the example at the command line in Ipython and it actually crashed the kernel!



There is also a test comparing expected coefficients for `db3`.  This test is written to compare 14 decimals and it looks like backing off to 11 decimals would make it pass.  Not sure why this would have changed, though?
```

issue_title: [D415] Sync issue after updating to SDK 2.10.X
Depth data/alignment sometimes corrupted on camera startup with OpenMP off (.NET)
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D400 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.08.15.00 | 
| Operating System & Version |   Win 10 1709 | 
| Platform                                 | PC, .NET, x64 |
| SDK Version                            |  2.10.0 compiled without OpenMP  | 

Hi,
I compiled the DLL without OpenMP to fix the high CPU usage problem.
However, when using this version of the DLL (the precompiled version doesn't show this behaviour) I get some strange behaviour when starting up the streams.
Firstly, this is what I should see when the camera is streaming:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8899900/36198062-7f123882-116d-11e8-9b63-4dc6605d7d52.png)
The image is coloured by the (aligned) depth. If the depth of a pixel is within a pre-defined range it's normal colour, if it's outside of that range but still has depth data it's tinted red, and if there's no depth data (z = 0) it's coloured dark grey.

Sometimes when starting the streams (the code to start the camera is always the same; I'm using 1920x1080 colour and 848x480 depth) I get the following:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8899900/36198160-b8937706-116d-11e8-9b2d-fd6dd62ac6b0.png)
Depth data is all zero. Usually stopping the camera, waiting a few seconds, then starting it again will fix this.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8899900/36198182-cb83b664-116d-11e8-9a84-25e4fee989db.png)
Alignment is out. Usually if it starts doing this, I need to restart the app completely to get it normal again.

One of the above two scenarios happens probably about 50% of the time I start the camera. The following two are much rarer than that.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8899900/36198279-0925e6b8-116e-11e8-9f87-27b7c237717d.png)
A small line of depth data in the centre of the image.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8899900/36198306-1ed10eb6-116e-11e8-854f-036fba0e68f4.png)
Alignment completely out and lots of poor depth areas.

I initially thought this may be due to how I'm closing the camera as it seems to happen more often when closing the camera (not the app) and starting it again within a few seconds, but it occasionally happens the first time the app is opened too. I shut the camera down by calling `pipeline.Stop()` and `pipeline.Dispose()`. Is there anything else I should be doing? Does disposing the pipeline also dispose of all child objects or do I need to dispose of everything individually? Or is this just one of the issues that having OpenMP enabled solves?
As I said, using the standard DLL fixes this issue but CPU usage goes to around 80-90% vs around 20% with openMP off so I'd rather have it turned off if possible to support lower-powered devices.

Thanks,
James

issue_title: Piezo and elastic data sets
issue_body:
@kylebystrom 

I made some changes to the "datasets" package of matminer. Some require your attention:

- Note that git-lfs seemed to be a big hassle and so I decided to just remove it altogether. Hopefully a normal "pull" operation will just fix everything on your end, but if not you may need to re-clone. Let's skip git-lfs from now on unless it really seems necessary, sorry for the hassle but now we know.
- Note that I added tests to the package
- Note that I added a note of the original reference both in the CSV and method
- **Needs your attention**: The elastic_tensor dataset is nice in that it contains both formula and structure columns that can be used to generate descriptors. However, the "piezo" and "dielectric_constant" data sets don't have these columns (they are embedded in "meta"). Can you pull these columns out of meta, along with anything else that looks useful to have as its own column? You'll need to update the unit tests afterward. Make sure to preserve the comment line at top that gives the reference.

issue_title: Capitalization consistency in feature names
issue_body:
Hi all

I'd like to make the capitalization of feature names in matminer consistent. We can do:

* everything lower case (e.g. "band center") except for acroynms (e.g. "formation energy FERE") 
* first-word capitalization ("Formation Energy FERE")

I'm leaning towards the former, it's just easier to remember and type. The latter leads to some people deciding to do "Formation energy FERE" which is not the same ...

@WardLT @JFChen3 @nisse3000 

Thoughts, comments, etc?

issue_body: Any way to allow access to classes/templates defined in py/hoc files - as they were written - ? Some member functions may be useful...

issue_title: Table Does Not Exist in Database Exception when table exists!!
issue_body:
**Background**

While trying to access a table in dashDB instance using the code:
```
from ibmdbpy import IdaDataBase, IdaDataFrame

idadb_1 = IdaDataBase(dsn='DASHDB;Database=BLUDB;Hostname=`<hostname>`;Port=50000;PROTOCOL=TCPIP;UID=`<UID>`;PWD=`<PASSWORD>`')
ida_df_1 = IdaDataFrame(idadb_1, 'schema.target_table')
ida_df_1.head()
```
, the user gets an exception complaining about that the table doesn't exist in the database.

When executing the command:
```
idadb_1.show_tables()
```

It clearly shows that the table is in the DB and under the selected schema. It also shows that the user (entered as `UID` previously) is the owner of the table.

issue_title: Assert that two publishes do not modify the RPM repodata
issue_body:
Test setup:
- Create a repo and sync+publish it (ie: zoo)

The test:
- Get the contents of repodata from the published repo
- publish zoo a second time
- Get the contents of repodata from the published repo now

Assert that all the metadata files and their filenames are identical.

If Pulp is publishing a second time, when there is nothing to do, it should do nothing. In the past a republish caused metadata to get republished in all cases which changes the checksum included in the filename. That was undesirable since the repodata itself didn't change the checksums shouldn't change.

This was believed to already be fixed so there isn't an open issue. For now this guarantee is only expected to be on RPM content, but I think it makes sense for all content types.

issue_title: Using the same type for querying and mutation
issue_body:
Today I learned that to allow a custom type as mutation argument, it needs to inherit from `InputObjectType` and it's fields need to be of `InputField` type. 

I think this requirement is forcing duplicating of types. What I mean is, say I have a `Person` type as:

```
class Person(ObjectType):
    id = Int()
    first_name = String()
    last_name = String()
    address = Field(SomeOtherType)
```

I expose it as a query-able object as well as return it as a result.

Now, If I want to allow it as a mutation argument to update a Person's details, I will have to redefine it as something else:

```
class Person_1(InputObjectType):
    id = Int()
    first_name = String()
    last_name = String()
    address = InputField(SomeOtherType)
```

If this is the only way to do this, I think it is forcing me to define near duplicate types. Is there a way to work around this?

Can something be done about this in the code so that we can use the same type definition for querying as well as a mutation argument?

issue_title: The one2many relationship would return all records in db if no specified
request not passed to filterclass
issue_body:
given the following code:
```
class ManageQuery(AbstractType):
    group_requests = DjangoFilterConnectionField(RequestNode, filterset_class=RequestManageFilter)
```
and the filterset class:
```
class RequestManageFilter(FilterSet):
    # Do case-insensitive lookups on 'name'

    @property
    def qs(self):
        user = getattr(self.request, 'user', None)
        print('User: ', user)
        parent = super(RequestManageFilter, self).qs
        return parent # .filter(group=self.request.user.main_author)

    class Meta:
        model = Request
        exclude = set()
```

the instance property self.request is always None, because when it's instantiated, graphene-django does not pass the context as request, shown here in fields.py's connection_resolver

```
   @classmethod
    def connection_resolver(cls, resolver, connection, default_manager, max_limit,
                            enforce_first_or_last, filterset_class, filtering_args,
                            root, args, context, info):
        filter_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in args.items() if k in filtering_args}

        qs = filterset_class(
            data=filter_kwargs,
            queryset=default_manager.get_queryset(),
        ).qs
```

passing request=context would make it work:
```
        qs = filterset_class(
            data=filter_kwargs,
            queryset=default_manager.get_queryset(),
            request=context,
        ).qs
```

issue_title: Re-enable explicit control of summary display name
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/6150

The basic issue is:
It used to be possible to precisely control the display name (or tag) of summary data in TensorBoard, because users had direct control over the tag field.

However, this meant that summary code ignored tf name scopes when generating summaries and tags. This was frustrating because it meant the following code was broken:

```
def layer(input):
  W = get_weights_variable(somehow)
  b = get_bias_variable(somehow)
  activation = tf.nn.relu(input * W + b)
  s = tf.summary.histogram("activation_histogram", activation)
  return activation

input = tf.placeholder(...)
layer1 = layer(input)
layer2 = layer(layer1)
...
merged = tf.summary.merge_all()
....
```

This code would be broken because the tag "activation_histogram" would be used twice, creating a conflict.

To fix this, the summary module (which was added in tf1.0) removes explicit control of the tag from the user. Instead, the first argument to the summary is the "name", and the generated node_name is used as the summary tag. As discussed in the linked issue, however, this creates its own set of frustrations and headaches.

I think a robust solution would be to add a display_name argument to summaries, which is used to create a SummaryMetadata that TensorBoard parses. Then, TensorBoard can use the display_name as the default name in tb, and fall back to the tag in case of conflicts.

I'm not sure how to implement this in a way that generalizes to the plugin system, though. I don't want to fix it just for individual plugins. We also need to continue to support the case where users manually generate the summary proto with an explicit tag.

@jart @wchargin  @chihuahua thoughts?

issue_title: Proposal: napalm library should abstract away internal files and directory structure
issue_body:
There are two fundamental ways that __init__.py can be used:

1. As a way to enable directory traversal and with some very minimal level of code definition.

2. As a method of hiding the internal directory structure and file names of a library (or some meaningful part thereof).

There is disagreement in the Python community on how __init__.py should be used. Some projects use only item 1; others use item 1 and 2.

I researched some various large Python projects yesterday and here were some results:

### Largely use __init__.py for only directory traversal and minimal code definiton
twisted
pytest
boto

### Use __init__.py for directory traversal and abstracting library internals
requests
flask
django (somewhat but it wrapped things inside a setup() function and was still pretty minimal)
paramiko

I also looked at lxml also but it was integrating to C libraries.
 

I am pretty strongly in favor that we should use napalm/__init__.py to abstract away the internal library directory names and file names. In principle, this is exactly the same issue that the NAPALM library itself was created for. 

Basically, if we do not abstract these away, then are directories and file names become a part of our programming interface. This results in it being difficult for us to change library internals. It also makes it more likely for us to break code for users that are using our code.

Here is a first pass at the set of things that I think should be in __init__.py:

```
from napalm.base import get_network_driver
from napalm._SUPPORTED_DRIVERS import SUPPORTED_DRIVERS

from napalm.base.base import NetworkDriver
from napalm.base.exceptions import ReplaceConfigException, MergeConfigException, \
            ConnectionClosedException, CommandErrorException

from napalm.base.utils import py23_compat  # noqa
from napalm.base import constants       # noqa
from napalm.base import helpers     # noqa
```

Here is a test repo where I made the changes also to the IOS driver. 

https://github.com/ktbyers/napalm/commit/2a7c2bef5caf038c62c16ce8a24d7a0b0a5d59e0

I am less concerned about changing internal references. I view this as largely optional (as we can always change internal references at a later date without breaking the code of any library users).

I don't think this has a meaningful impact on memory size as `get_network_driver` will load napalm.base.__init__ which will load these things anyways. If we want to improve memory usage then we should figure out a way to not generally import large external libraries (in cases where they are not used).

### As part of this we should ensure that it is backwards compatible with Salt, StackStorm2, Ansible, and Netbox (if we do the above). 

Basically we should ensure that nothing here breaks backwards compatibility with other major tools that rely on us. I think this can be accomplished without much above what is required for reunification.

### Why it matters now
Once we tell people to update the code to the new reunified structure and how to do that...we are exceptionally unlikely to ask them to do this again. This will potentially start publishing library internals to end-users and as more and more users rely on these things...it will be harder and harder to change.

issue_title: Deep residual block always uses a projection instead of identity
issue_body:
Refering to this line: https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/tflearn/layers/conv.py#L617.
Because it is connected to the two high-dimensional ends, it doubles model size and the time to train a residual net according to He _et al._ . 

I guess you want to do this instead:

```python
# Projection to new dimension
                if in_channels != out_channels:
                    identity = conv_2d(identity, out_channels, 1, 1, 'same',
                                       'linear', bias, weights_init,
                                       bias_init, regularizer, weight_decay,
                                       trainable, restore)
```

I can submit a PR if you want.

issue_title: Questions regarding the embeddings produced by the `skipgram` and `supervised` options
issue_body:
Hello!

As far as I understand `fastText` is implementing two research papers [1, 2] and both papers can be used to learn word embeddings:

- [1] learns the embeddings by predicting the current word from its surrounding character n-grams
- [2] learns word embeddings that are specifically geared towards a classification task

A few questions:

1. Given that both systems have an embedding component, I was wondering whether: (i) you tried to perform the classification task on the skip-gram embeddings from [1]; (ii) you could modify the architecture in [2] to work on character n-grams.

2. In [2] you are averaging word embeddings to obtain the embedding of a sentence. Does averaging make sense for the skip-gram embeddings from [1] as well? More generally, when is it a good idea to average embeddings in order to obtain the embedding of a larger chunk of text? This question might be related to #26.

3. The help function suggests that the two parts of the code (`skipgram` and `supervised`) use the same arguments. Is this right? Do you use character n-grams for `supervised` (the `minn` and `maxn` options) or n-gram words for `skipgram` (the `wordNgram` option)?

Thanks!

[1] P. Bojanowski, E. Grave, A. Joulin, T. Mikolov, Enriching Word Vectors with Subword Information
[2] A. Joulin, E. Grave, P. Bojanowski, T. Mikolov, Bag of Tricks for Efficient Text Classification

issue_title: function<Type*(void)> will attempt to copy Type on return
GCC 7.1 (mingw-w64) has broken -flto and hypot
issue_body:
For future:
GCC 7.1 (mingw-w64)
Just examine problem while compiling tests with unreferenced symbols on `Release` and `MinSizeRel` . 
`-flto` have mystic conflicts with -O3 or -Os 
Temp solution while gcc developers not fix bug: 
Disable -flto flag like this:
`cmake ..  -G"Ninja" -DPYBIND11_LTO_CXX_FLAGS=""`

mingw-w64 headers have conflict with python headers: 
`error: '::hypot' has not been declared`
we can fix it with redefining `-D_hypot=hypot`
`cmake ..  -G"Ninja" -DPYBIND11_LTO_CXX_FLAGS="" -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="-D_hypot=hypot"`

issue_body:
Hi,
i rad into something weird today. Apparently I have some "deleted" calendar entries that google marks cancelled. They show up in the log with " warning: Skipping '/caldav/v2/name@domain.com/events/blahbblub_R20170418T150000@google.com.ics', properties are missing.
After that vdirsyncer stops with 
```
error: Unknown error occured for s2_to_ce/work: /caldav/v2/name@domain/events/iblahblub@google.com.ics
error: Use `-vdebug` to see the full traceback.
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/cli/tasks.py", line 69, in sync_collection
debug:     partial_sync=pair.partial_sync
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 563, in sync
debug:     a_nonempty = a_info.prepare_new_status()
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 487, in prepare_new_status
debug:     if prefetch else ()):
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py", line 500, in get_multi
debug:     raise exceptions.NotFoundError(href)
error: 1 out of 2 tasks failed.
```

After digging around in the code I patched it vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py:
```                                                                                                                              
diff --git a/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py b/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
index 7a3e6ee..b8fa99f 100644
--- a/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
+++ b/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
@@ -497,7 +497,8 @@ class DAVStorage(Storage):
             else:
                 rv.append((href, Item(raw), etag))
         for href in hrefs_left:
-            raise exceptions.NotFoundError(href)
+            dav_logger.warning('Could not get {}'.format(href))
+            #raise exceptions.NotFoundError(hrefs_left)
         return rv
 
     def _put(self, href, item, etag):
```

I do not claim to fully understand the concept behind hrefs_left and why its needed. But I do think raising the exception may not be the right thing here. I replaced it with a warning. Now I can finally sync :-)

issue_title: exceptions.NotFoundError(hrefs_left) causes sync to abort instead announced skip
issue_body:
Hi,
i rad into something weird today. Apparently I have some "deleted" calendar entries that google marks cancelled. They show up in the log with " warning: Skipping '/caldav/v2/name@domain.com/events/blahbblub_R20170418T150000@google.com.ics', properties are missing.
After that vdirsyncer stops with 
```
error: Unknown error occured for s2_to_ce/work: /caldav/v2/name@domain/events/iblahblub@google.com.ics
error: Use `-vdebug` to see the full traceback.
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/cli/tasks.py", line 69, in sync_collection
debug:     partial_sync=pair.partial_sync
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 563, in sync
debug:     a_nonempty = a_info.prepare_new_status()
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/sync.py", line 487, in prepare_new_status
debug:     if prefetch else ()):
debug:   File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py", line 500, in get_multi
debug:     raise exceptions.NotFoundError(href)
error: 1 out of 2 tasks failed.
```

After digging around in the code I patched it vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py:
```                                                                                                                              
diff --git a/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py b/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
index 7a3e6ee..b8fa99f 100644
--- a/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
+++ b/vdirsyncer/storage/dav.py
@@ -497,7 +497,8 @@ class DAVStorage(Storage):
             else:
                 rv.append((href, Item(raw), etag))
         for href in hrefs_left:
-            raise exceptions.NotFoundError(href)
+            dav_logger.warning('Could not get {}'.format(href))
+            #raise exceptions.NotFoundError(hrefs_left)
         return rv
 
     def _put(self, href, item, etag):
```

I do not claim to fully understand the concept behind hrefs_left and why its needed. But I do think raising the exception may not be the right thing here. I replaced it with a warning. Now I can finally sync :-)

issue_title: certonly with webroot plugin: Cannot create multiple certificates with one command
issue_body:
Hello!

Following the [documentation here](https://certbot.eff.org/docs/using.html#webroot) for the webroot plugin, it appears that I should be able to create multiple certificates in one go. Specifically. I have two sites with two web roots.

The example given in the docs:

> [Taken from docs:] When requesting a certificate for multiple domains, each domain will use the most recently specified --webroot-path. So, for instance,

```bash
certbot certonly --webroot -w /var/www/example/ -d www.example.com -d example.com -w /var/www/other -d other.example.net -d another.other.example.net
```

## Reproduce

* System: Ubuntu 16.04 x64, brand new server (DigitalOcean droplet)
* Nginx 1.10.0
* Python 2.7.11 (although I'm not sure what certbot uses with the virtualenv it creates)

I've tried both latest `master` and release `v0.8.1` - here's the commands I run to create two new certificates:

```bash
# Use root
sudo su

# clone repo and use certbot
cd /opt
git clone https://github.com/certbot/certbot
cd /opt/certbot
git checkout v0.8.1

# create two certs
# note these webroots do exist and work (DNS is set)
./certbot-auto certonly --webroot \
    -w /var/www/one.example.io -d one.example.io \
    -w /var/www/two.example.io -d two.example.io \
    --non-interactive --agree-tos --email me@example.io
```

## Result

Everything seems to run smoothly, however only the first certificate is created.

```
IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - Congratulations! Your certificate and chain have been saved at
   /etc/letsencrypt/live/one.example.io/fullchain.pem....
 - If you lose your account credentials...
 - Your account credentials have been saved...
 - If you like Certbot...

    Donating ...

```

(No output regarding the second webroot or domain)

**Only one certificate is created (the first listed). The second is ignored, and there is no related output.**

This is the same for the `renew` command as well as both `certonly` both ignore the second webroot.


Let me know if it looks like I'm in error or if I can send along any other information!

issue_title: Accept domains containing underscores as FQDNs
issue_body:
I understand that domains containing underscores are a violation of one or more RFC specs, but all major browsers support them, all popular DNS providers support them, and some software needs them for "magic subdomains" that start with a leading underscore.

I'm trying to get a certificate for a domain that's in the format of `_something.example.org`, which some software needs.

issue_title: NFC data transfer too slow using nfcpy
issue_body:
Hi guys,
I have written a python application, based on nfcpy, that transfers a binary file onto a type 2 tag.
So far everything worked fine besides the transfer speed. On windows the transfer rate is around 200Bytes/second and on Linux slightly better, 280Bytes/second whereas I would expect a closer values to the theoretical value of 800Bytes/second.
I have stripped completely my python script and my Tag code, so basically I can't blame any of the two applications. 
When debugging I do see that the messages take quite a while to write, around 0.8 seconds per record, which is quite a lot. My records have 150 bytes.
So at this point I am inclined to blame the USB driver or the nfcpy which are the only black boxes in this setup.
I have used two different card readers, the Sony S380 and the ACR122, the results on the Sony are slightly better, maybe because it uses a newer version of the PN5XX (PN533) and the ACR122 uses the PN532.
Regarding the drivers I have been using the native drivers, which don't work, and the lisbusb v1.2.6.0, libusbkcv3.0.7.0 which give the above results.
On Linux, the performance increases a bit, to around 250bytes/second, still far from the intended speed.
Does someone have an idea how I can improve the transfer rate on my setup?

Any opinions on this pattern?

issue_title: bug 1331098 - use claimWork instead of hitting azure
issue_body:
Currently, we ask Taskcluster what the polling urls are, then poll Azure for the task hints, then `claimTask` the appropriate task(s)..

At some point, [claimWork](https://docs.taskcluster.net/reference/platform/queue/references/api#claimWork) is going to become the standard way of claiming tasks from the Taskcluster queue.  No worker currently uses this, but the TC team will test a busy docker-worker workerType against this soon.

A caveat here: we're considering supporting multiple workerTypes in scriptworker, to help autophone (#44).  If we use the claimWork endpoint, we may have to order the workerTypes (workerType B doesn't claim any work until all workerType A tasks are claimed) or randomize, or something.  If we have to support both workerTypes on an equal footing, we may have to stay on Azure.

issue_title: Enforce flake8 check in Travis
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Not all of us are properly following the styling rules of flake8. Flake8 is also a linter and can spot bugs like unused/uninitialized variables.

## Solution

Enforce flake8 in travis. But omit the long line rule, only in Travis, since some times this rule can and should be ignored. If a PR fails the flake8 test it sould be marked as "Failed" and the creator shoud fix it.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Ensure that no file on the codebase fails the above mentioned flake8 check
- [ ] Enable the flake8 check in travis and configure it as mandatory. i.e.: If it fails the build fails.

issue_title: Remove monkey patch hotfix for tinyrpc session IF upstream PR gets merged
issue_body:
Due to https://github.com/mbr/tinyrpc/issues/26 our rpc client (`pyethapp.rpc_client.JSONRPCClient`) was [prone to exhausting client ports](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/253).

I have created a [PR against the upstream (tinyrpc)](https://github.com/mbr/tinyrpc/pull/31) in order to address this. For the meantime, I have created a monkey patch (https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/350) to work around this.

Once the upstream PR is merged, or a similar patch is applied to `pyethapp`'s `JSONRPCClient`, we should get rid of the monkey patching.

issue_title: Docker in Windows: `run:` and `bash:` not working
issue_body:
I do not have a Windows machine, but I wonder if you've tried docker `run` and `bash` actions on Windows. Earlier I was asking someone to run LaTeX from my docker images on their Windows machine. But it did not work. With `run:`

```
[1]
run: container='gaow/debian-texlive'
    pandoc --help
```

I believe the error message was "exec permission denied", because the docker command looks like `docker run ... gaow/debian-texlive /var/lib/sos/xxxxx.bat`. That is, it did not call `bash` but instead tries to run the `bat` file by itself.

Then I asked him to try with `bash:`

```
[1]
bash: container='gaow/debian-texlive'
    pandoc --help
```

docker correctly used `/bin/bash` to run the command this time. But the error message was something like `xxx.sh: Is directory`. I suspect it is because the mount volume command argument, `-v .sos/script.sh:/var/lib/sos/script.sh` somehow makes docker think it is a directory? 

My questions: 

1. Can you reproduce those on Windows?
2. Why do we seem to map the script, not its directory, in `-v`?

WORKFLOW

issue_title: Change the way fast.ai is documented
issue_body:
Hey all! I recently had a look into documenting python libraries for a work related project. I combined that with digging a little deeper into the fast.ai library. I know that there's already some work done for the documentation and I don't mean to disrespect that, but I thought I share this as an alternative. [Here](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/index.html) you can find a build of the skeleton, which includes all the content from the current /docs directory + [my documentation of the transforms module](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/transforms.html). You can find the source in the /docs folder of [my fastai-fork](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/tree/master/docs).

I'm using sphinx with some extensions. Here's why I think it's a good approach to do it like that:

* **It's easy to get involved:** You don't have to care a lot about the documentation framework. It's almost entirely written in markdown (with some reStructuredText for the autogenerated docstrings). There's one markdown file per module in the [source folder](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/tree/master/docs/source).

* **It's possible and very easy to incorporate autogenerated docstrings.** Sphinx allows to use reStructuredText code blocks to incorporate docstrings. You can individually paste those blocks wherever you feel it's neccessary in the documentation. ([See](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/blob/master/docs/source/transforms.md) the source for the fastai.transforms module)

* **You can include ipython notebooks.** You just have to put the name in the [index.rst](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/blob/master/docs/source/index.rst) file. [As you can see](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/notebooks/TestNotebook.html) that's a pretty neat feature. So even if people do not dive deep into the library they can contribute examples and best practices to show how the library works. 

* **Flexible and easy building and deployment.** You can build the static html files from your fast.ai environment or with a [docker image](https://hub.docker.com/r/kailicht/fastai/~/dockerfile/) I created for this purpose (only one command: `docker run -it --rm -v ~/PathTo/local/fastai:/fastai kailicht/fastai:docs`). It's static html so can be deployed on github.pages, readthedocs (autodeployment) or just on the fast.ai webspace.


I really think that a good documentation is absolutely crucial, especially for projects like fast.ai. It's not only complex in terms of software engineering but also very scientific. Maybe this can be a good starting point for a documentation where people can easily get involved. It's a working skeleton with a framework that gives a lot of room to customize and improve.

@jph00 Let me know if you want me to make a pull request

issue_title: Remove or replace Redis client retry mechanism.
issue_body:
Right now, whenever we submit an asynchronous command to Redis (from a C++ client), we keep around enough information to resend the command. If we do not receive an acknowledgement from the Redis server within 10 seconds, we reissue the command. Then we wait another 10 seconds, and if we still haven't heard back, we reissue the command. And so on forever. A lot of the code for this is in `src/common/state/table.cc`. You can tell that this is happening because one of the log files will have messages like the following (see #561 for a workload that currently causes this problem)

```
[WARN] (/Users/rkn/Workspace/ray/src/common/state/table.cc:85) retrying operation task_table_test_and_update, retry_count = -1
```

Usually when this start happening, the system will not recover from it and will retry infinitely.

As far as I can tell, there are 0 situations where reissuing the Redis command is the right thing to do. Either the Redis client's connection to the Redis server is still alive, in which case the command will eventually go through just fine without any retrying, or the connection is been dropped in which case the connection needs to be reestablished, and retrying won't help with that (currently we don't have a way of reestablishing the connection).

For these reasons, I'd suggest removing the retry mechanism.

issue_title: [xray] Unable to create 0 CPU actor when there are no slots available.
Make use of __getstate__ and __setstate__  during serialization
issue_body:
<!--
General questions should be asked on the mailing list ray-dev@googlegroups.com.

Before submitting an issue, please fill out the following form.
-->

### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: Platform independent
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: pip install ray
- **Ray version**:  0.2.2
- **Python version**: 3.6.2
- **Exact command to reproduce**: See examples below

<!--
You can obtain the Ray version with

python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
-->

### Describe the problem
<!-- Describe the problem clearly here. -->

`__getstate__` and `__setstate__` is commonly used for serialization. But it seems that ray does not make use of them. 

Some objects may contain raw system objects (file handlers, etc.) or duplicated values (cache, temporary variable, etc.).  Trying to serialize they by directly expanding them as dictionaries of their fields could be incorrect and inefficient. `__getstate__` and `__setstate__` can also be used as workaround (users could override them) in case serialization fails.

### Source code / logs
<!-- Include any logs or source code that would be helpful to diagnose the problem. If including tracebacks, please include the full traceback. Large logs and files should be attached. Try to provide a reproducible test case that is the bare minimum necessary to generate the problem. -->

```python
# Example
class SomeClass:
    def __init__(self, filename):
        self.name = filename
        self.file = open(filename)
    
    def __getstate__(self):
        odict = self.__dict__.copy()  # get attribute dictionary
        del odict['file']  # remove filehandle entry
        return odict
    
    def __setstate__(self, dict):
        fh = open(dict['name'])  # reopen file
        self.name = dict['name']
        self.file = fh

obj = SomeClass('/dev/zero')
object_id = ray.put(obj)
res = ray.get(object_id)
res.file.read()  # ---> ValueError: I/O operation on uninitialized object

import pickle
res = pickle.loads(pickle.dumps(obj))
res.file.read()  # OK
```

* Package version: 1.7.2 * Django version: 2.1 * Python version: 3.6.1 ### Description: I have a basic Tag filter: ``` class TagFilter(django_filters.FilterSet): title = django_filters.CharFilter(lookup_expr='icontains') class Meta: form = TagFilterForm model = Tag fields = ['title'] ``` With a very basic ModelForm: ``` class TagFilterForm(ModelForm): class Meta: model = Tag fields = ('title',) def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs): super(TagFilterForm, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs) self.helper = FormHelper() self.helper.form_method = 'GET' self.helper.layout = Layout( Fieldset('

Filter

', 'title', FormActions( HTML("Cancel"), Submit('submit', 'Filter'), ), ), ) ``` that is rendered with a simple ListView: ``` class TagListView(LoginRequiredMixin, TagMixin, ListView): model = Tag template_name = 'pages/list.html' context_object_name = 'tag' ordering = ['id'] def get_context_data(self, **kwargs): context = super(TagListView, self).get_context_data(**kwargs) tag_filter = TagFilter(self.request.GET, queryset=self.get_queryset()) table = TagTable(tag_filter.qs) RequestConfig(self.request, paginate={'per_page': 10}).configure(table) context['filter'] = tag_filter context['form'] = tag_filter.form context['table'] = table context['user'] = self.request.user return context ``` using the most basic template code: ```
{% crispy form %}
``` This results in a very strange bug where the queryset text is rendered directly onto the page after the Fieldset header and before the `title` field. ![screen shot 2018-08-10 at 2 19 28 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4741454/43974594-0ec2516c-9ca9-11e8-85f6-74a9b0062271.png) ```
--   |     |     |

Filter

 <<QuerySet [<Tag: Tag 1>, <Tag: Tag2>, <Tag: Tag3>, <Tag: Tag4>, <Tag: Tag5>, <Tag: Test>]> id="div_id_title" class="form-group">
Cancel
  |
  |     |
I expected *not* to see the strange QuerySet text in my form. Is Tag a protected keyword? I have other models with this exact same code, just different model name, that work.

issue_title: Make HLS chunk-lists playable
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [x] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [x] This is an improvement request.
- [ ] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description

Instead of sending a playlist first some web sites immediately send chunk lists, like:

```
#EXTM3U
#EXT-X-VERSION:3
#EXT-X-TARGETDURATION:6
#EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE:666
#EXTINF:2.0,
video1.ts
#EXTINF:2.0,
video2.ts
#EXTINF:2.0,
video3.ts
```
Firefox addon ["Native HLS Playback"](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/native-hls-0-7-10/) by [Gaetan Hervouet](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/user/graamk/) has no issue playing such files.

Streamlink gives an error: "No playable streams found on this URL: ..."

Hope it's possible to fix.

issue_title: Twitch Streams not supporting --hls-start-offset of >30 seconds?
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [x] This is a bug report.
- [ ] This is a feature request.
- [ ] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description

When using --hls-start-offset on a twitch live stream, it only respects values up to 30 seconds.

### Expected / Actual behavior

Expected behavior: Twitch Live stream starts with desired offset of 60 seconds.
Actual behavior: Twitch Live stream starts with offset of 30 seconds.

### Reproduction steps / Explicit stream URLs to test

streamlink https://www.twitch.tv/sodapoppin "720p,480p,best" --hls-segment-threads 10 --hls-start-offset 00:01:00 --stream-priority "hls,hds,rtmp,http,akamaihd,*" --loglevel debug --force --output fetch.mp4

After that (segment 6224-6226), streamlink waits and occasionally downloads new segments. I exited the program at segment 6226 manually, then checked the saved video file for its duration: only 36 seconds (instead of >60 seconds).

The same commands works fine on other livestreaming sites, going back in time for 60 seconds at the start.

`ProgrammingError at /helpdesk/dashboard/

The error was found at these lines in `staff.py`:
`                    COUNT(CASE t.status WHEN '1' THEN t.id WHEN '2' THEN t.id END) AS open,
                    COUNT(CASE t.status WHEN '3' THEN t.id END) AS resolved,
                    COUNT(CASE t.status WHEN '4' THEN t.id END) AS closed
            %s
            %s
            GROUP BY queue, name
            ORDER BY q.id;
    """ % (from_clause, where_clause)) `

issue_title: Update HGVS Parsing
issue_body: It wasn't worth trying to parse these. Just assume that they're valid HGVS strings and store them accordingly. In the future, other modules such as https://github.com/mutalyzer/mutalyzer or https://github.com/counsyl/hgvs

issue_title: Testing on custom namespaces for molecular activities
issue_body: This will also be a problem, but more likely to occur. It would be good to use GO Molecular Functions as a namespace when doing this.

issue_title: Allow proxy host configuration on per-host configuration entries
issue_body: Currently the per-host configuration provided by `host_config` does not allow for proxy host configuration.

issue_title: Widget Requests
issue_body:
# Widget Requests
Not sure where to ask, but I have been making my own custom widgets using cookie cutter template and figured it would be good to at least ask for some of them to be added for all.  These may seem trivial, but I have found them to be quite useful.

## Widgets using Cookie Cutter:
**LinkButton Widget**
Pretty straight forward.  Could be used as a redirect, or what I prefer a small footprint button click.  

**BreadCrumbs Widget** 
Basically a collection of LinkButton Widgets that grow horizontally with a list of values separated by a character.  Separator character is an argument with a default of '/'.  Would be nice if it rendered HTML unicode characters.

**BusyIndicator Widget** 
Basically an animated gif to indicate something is happening.  Visibility is controlled with an enabled property.  I hard coded the base-64 image right into the widget.

**Improved RadioButtons**
I needed to arrange radio buttons horizontally, couldn't seem to accomplish this with existing widget in 5.2.2 so I made my own.  Not sure if this has been addressed in 6.0...

## Widget Classes Made from Existing Widgets:

**ListTab**
Similar function to a tab container, except the content is selected by a single selected item in a Select widget positioned left of the container.  This is useful for views of many items that would be too excessive for the tab control.  I currently use this to load up many plotly plots and allow users to "scroll" through them.

**SelectTab**
Similar to the ListTab but not the same.  Uses a dropdown to select the current content, but the dropdown is above the content in a VBox, and it allows space to the right of the dropdown for a toolbar of buttons that can be used to perform action on the content.  I use this to look at different views of the same data and the buttons are used to expand the view or save the plot as an image. 

### Wishlist that I am working on still:
**CheckBoxSelect Widget** 
I have failed to be able to come with this one, but my users would really like to have this ability.

**UploadBox**
This one is stalled as well.  Would add ability to handle a drag drop event for uploading files from within the notebook output.  Having to upload files outside the notebook through Jupyter is clunky for my users. Would basically add a drag-over and drop event to whatever children it contains.

## Haven't Found a Way to Deploy Cookie Cutter Widgets to Dashboard Server
Some of my motivation for asking for these requests is that we have been unable to get our jupyter hub dashboard server operational due to not being able to load up custom widgets.  I do not know all the details of that issue, but I am told it has something to do with the js require not finding the custom widget js.  Sounds like we need a jupyter nbextension enable for dashboard servers?  

Anyways if the cookie cutter widgets above were part of the ipywidgets library then I wouldn't even need our custom js widgets.

Well, there you go, all my dirty laundry out there for everyone to pick apart why I did it that way.  If there are other controls out there readily available, please let me know.  

If you got this far, thanks!

Regards,

-Randy

issue_title: perhaps a ipywidget/lab cookiecutter?
issue_body:
When l was learning to write ipywidgets for the notebook  I found that

````https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/widget-cookiecutter```` 

was very helpful.  As best I can tell, by studying ````ipyleaflet/js/src/jupyterlab-plugin.js````  at least one extra configuration file is needed for jupyterlab beyond those created by the widget cookiecutter.  This additional file seems to register one's new widget with jlab.   

The absence of this file may be responsible for the error message

    Error displaying widget

mentioned also in issue [1912](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/issues/1912).

I found a couple of new-ish and somewhat related cookiecutters:
 - https://github.com/jupyterlab/extension-cookiecutter-js
 - https://github.com/jupyterlab/extension-cookiecutter-ts

but these do not address ipywidgets.

Perhaps a slightly expanded cookiecutter for ipython lab widgets could be cobbled together by someone who knows what he or she is doing?

Thanks for all the great work on jupyterlab!

 - Paul

issue_title: Dataset add should not attempt to auto-match lineage datasets if they exist in db
issue_body:
When adding datasets to the database `datacube dataset add` does roughly following

- For every input dataset
   1. Auto-match dataset to a product (fail if not found)
   2. For every dataset in the lineage of an input dataset
         1. Auto-match dataset to the product (fail if not found)

If you are indexing derived dataset with lineage that already exists in the database, then second auto-match is not necessary, those datasets are already in the database, and are already associated to a product. 

This behaviour makes `--dtype` option behave in a way that most users won't expect, when indexing datasets with lineage. Here is a help string for this option:

```
  -t, --dtype TEXT                Product to be associated with the datasets
```

Several problems already

1. Using `dtype` name that is highly associated with `numpy.dtype` in the target community, but is not *that kind of dtype*
2. Actually means `dataset-type` == `product`
3. Inconsistent naming on one line: *"dataset type"* on a command line, *"product"* in the help string
4. You can actually specify multiple products, help doesn't make this clear

What this option really is: 

> Restrain auto-matching to a subset of products from the database (you can supply several on a command line), auto-matching is applied to the new datasets listed on the command line and any lineage datasets referenced in the metadata.

### User experience scenario

1. Created some new datasets using stats tool or similar (so they have lineage data)
2. Added product definition for my new datasets
3. Tried running with default options and was told that my datasets could match to several products in the DB and so no indexing can proceed
   - `datacube product add` will not warn you when adding products with "too similar" metadata
4. No worries I can specify product with this weirdly named `--dtype` option
5. Try running `datacube dataset add -t my_new_product my_new_dataset.nc`, still get an error

But I just told you which product to use!!?

The problem is that now that you have restrained set of products to match against to just one product you care about, you can't auto-match any of the lineage products at all. 

But you should not need to, those datasets are already in the DB, no need to auto-match them in this case. Sure you still need to auto-match IF and ONLY IF lineage datasets are not in the DB already.

### Conclusion

Combination of poor documentation and decision to auto-match before checking DB makes `--dtype` option unusable to anyone but those who are ultimately familiar with the implementation of `datacube dataset add` command.

issue_title: Drop task management from trio.Queue
issue_body:
Specifically this means deprecating and removing `task_done` and `join`.

I've been tempted to do this for a while b/c they're complicated, very rarely useful (as in: I'm not sure they have any use at all in Trio), and deeply inelegant (why is there an independent synchronization mechanism bolted onto `Queue`?). The only reason they're there in the first place is to match stdlib `queue.Queue`. Final straw: https://bugs.python.org/issue14976#msg301173

issue_title: Configuration set via Configuration.set_default doesn't seem to be respected (6.0.0)
issue_body:
I'm here by way of [this Ansible issue ](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/issues/43845) and an inability to connect to to Openshift using a self-signed cert while specifying `verify_ssl`=`False`.

I think I've narrowed it down to the k8s python module not respecting how the Ansible module is [setting the default config](https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/module_utils/k8s/common.py#L158).

I put together this test to illustrate:

`test.py`:
```python
import yaml
import kubernetes
from kubernetes import client, config
from openshift.dynamic import DynamicClient

configuration = kubernetes.client.Configuration()
setattr(configuration, 'verify_ssl', False)
kubernetes.client.Configuration.set_default(configuration)

k8s_client = config.new_client_from_config()
dyn_client = DynamicClient(k8s_client)
v1_services = dyn_client.resources.get(api_version='v1', kind='Service')

service = """
kind: Project
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: testssl
"""

service_data = yaml.load(service)
resp = v1_services.create(body=service_data, namespace='default')

# resp is a ResourceInstance object
print(resp.metadata)
```

Adding debug statements in `kube_config.py`:
```python
...
def new_client_from_config(
        config_file=None,
        context=None,
        persist_config=True):
    """Loads configuration the same as load_kube_config but returns an ApiClient
    to be used with any API object. This will allow the caller to concurrently
    talk with multiple clusters."""
    client_config = type.__call__(Configuration)
    print('kube_config.new_client_from_config Configuration._default: ' + str(Configuration._default.verify_ssl))
    print('kube_config.new_client_from_config client_config:: ' + str(client_config.verify_ssl))
    load_kube_config(config_file=config_file, context=context,
                     client_configuration=client_config,
                     persist_config=persist_config)
    return ApiClient(configuration=client_config)
```
Result:
```$ python test.py
kube_config.new_client_from_config Configuration._default: False
kube_config.new_client_from_config client_config:: True
verify_ssl true
2018-08-14 19:01:38,955 WARNING Retrying (Retry(total=2, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLError(1, u'[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:579)'),)': /version
```

Is this something that should be working and isn't, or am I (and Ansible) doing something wrong to configure the defaults? (I'm not a Python dev, so I don't quite grok how [the defaults are supposed to work](https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/master/kubernetes/client/configuration.py#L27))

Hello, 

We're on an auto-updating :frowning: instance of Gitlab and it's up to date with 9.3, which just came out yesterday. Two days ago, I created 14 projects without a problem, today it's complaining about something to do with the webhooks. We haven't changed anything in our project creation process, it's been the same since we started using your awesome library in February. 

Here are the keys of our what the webhooks set up - 

```
  webhooks:
    url: [redacted]
    # What we want the webhook to talk about.
    build_events: False
    enable_ssl_verification: True
    issues_events: True
    merge_requests_events: True
    note_events: False
    pipeline_events: True
    push_events: False
    tag_push_events: False

issue_title: Changing file-extension for standalone grammar definitions from .g?
issue_body:
Currently standalone files like [common](https://github.com/erezsh/lark/blob/master/lark/grammars/common.g) and the example [json](https://github.com/erezsh/lark/blob/master/examples/standalone/json.g) use the file extension `.g`. However, it looks like `g` is [already associated](http://filext.com/file-extension/G) with the [ANTLR](http://www.antlr.org/) parser. While I suppose it's possible to make Lark compatible with ANTLR, in the meantime it's probably best to use a different file extension. I would propose the extension `lrk` as it [doesn't seem](http://filext.com/file-extension/lrk) to be used by anything currently. 

I'm about to submit a pull request to change the file extensions to `.lrk` in the relevant file names and in the code referencing the `.g` extension. It's on a separate branch so if you want to use a different extension that should be easy enough to change.

issue_title: Exposes "submit" method on form elements
issue_body:
If I have a form, like in:

    from splinter.browser import Browser
    browser = Browser()
    browser.visit('http://www.google.com/')
    my_form = browser.find_by_tag('form').first

the object `my_form._element` will have the `submit` method.

As we should not use `_element` since it is related to Selenium, Splinter should exposes a `submit` method in this form-object, so we can submit forms without needing to search for the submit button (or element that actually submits the form).

It is not useful when we want to test if some button, when clicked, submits the form correctly. But it's very useful when we just want to submit the form and check what is the response (and don't want to know the name of the button/element that submits the form).

```
def test_reverse(self):
        class DBColumn(models.Model):
            a = models.IntegerField(db_column='b')
        model1 = DBColumn.objects.create(a=1)
        model2 = DBColumn.objects.create(a=2)
        self.assertEqual(list(DBColumn.objects.all().order_by('a').reverse()), [model2, model1])
        self.assertEqual(list(DBColumn.objects.all().order_by('-a').reverse()), [model1, model2])
